attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) on a cron schedule or once at a specific time. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress



## Todo

A markdown list of tasks to do in the near future, ordered
highest-priority first. Keep entries brief — 1-3 lines.
Detailed motivation, safety requirements, and ordering belong
in `notes/chores-NN.md` design subsections; link via `[N]` ref.

Items use lazy numbering — every entry begins with `1. `; the
markdown renderer auto-numbers them, so reorder/insert without
renumbering. Reference by displayed number ("let's work on #3").

1. vc-x1 commands: support single and dual repo. Generalize via
   `--scope=app|other|both` across all commands; foundational
   for new commands and for retrofitting sync / push / finalize
   under one vocabulary. [60]
1. vc-x1 push: `--scope=app|claude|both` flag. Applies the
   generalized convention; warn on scope/WC mismatch. [57],[60]
1. vc-x1 push: `--squash` flag. Squashes WC into `@-` via
   `--ignore-immutable` and force-pushes; needs
   `--force-with-lease`-equivalent + state-sanity preflight in
   place first. [57]
1. vc-x1 push: `--message-file PATH` flag. Git-style commit
   message file (first line = title, blank, rest = body).
   Alternative to `--title` + `--body`. [58]
1. Mirror `--check` / `--no-check` onto `vc-x1 push` (forwards
   through to the preflight `vc-x1 sync` invocation).
   0.37.1 hard-codes `--check`; default stays `--check`.
1. Add `status` (alias `st`) subcommand: `jj st` across both
   repos in one shot. Uses `--scope` from day one. Natural
   home for the working-copy signal called out in [54].
1. Add `validate-repo` subcommand: diagnostic that runs all
   `verify_*` checks (tracking, push state freshness, ochid
   integrity, conflicts, config sanity, working-copy state)
   and reports per-check pass/fail. Exit code = number of
   failed checks. Implementation: promote
   `verify_state_sanity` / `verify_completion_sanity` from
   push.rs to `common.rs`. [69]
1. sync: surface working-copy state in the up-to-date summary
   (per-repo pending-files count or compact stat). Wording-only
   fix shipped in 0.37.1; this is the design+impl. [54]
1. bm-track silent-when-clean refinement. Print on entry/exit
   only when state isn't fully tracked or when exit state
   differs from entry. [62]
1. "Oh shit" revert — post-success undo via `.vc-x1-ops/`
   anchor dir. Idea-stage; every repo-mutating command drops a
   pre-op snapshot, `vc-x1 undo` restores both repos. [57]
1. vc-x1 test-fixture should refuse `--path` values that resolve
   inside the current workspace root (error or warn). Dogfood
   surfaced this: `--path ./tf-1` inside the repo let jj
   snapshot the fixture's bare-git remotes — 56-file noise blob.
1. Restructure templates: replace separate `vc-template-x1` +

content.text:
Acquainted. Here's the lay of the land:

**Project**: `vc-x1` — a Rust CLI (v0.39.0, edition 2024) wrapping `jj` for dual-repo workflows (app repo + `.claude` session repo). Last release "push hardening cycle" just landed on main.

**Source** (`src/`): subcommands `chid`, `clone`, `desc`, `finalize`, `fix_desc`, `init`, `list`, `push`, `show`, `symlink`, `sync`, `validate_desc`, `test_fixture` + helpers (`common`, `desc_helpers`, `logging`, `toml_simple`, `test_helpers`).

**Working copy**:
- App repo: clean, `@` empty on `main` at `f6369792`.
- `.claude` repo: active session writes (expected — per CLAUDE.md, `.claude` always has uncommitted changes during a session).

**Todo queue** (top of `notes/todo.md`): `--scope=app|other|both` generalization is the big foundational item; several `push` flags queued behind it (`--squash`, `--message-file`, mirrored `--check`/`--no-check`); plus `status`/`st` and `validate-repo` subcommands, bm-track refinement, and an "oh shit" revert idea.

**Key guardrails from CLAUDE.md** I'm holding:
- `-R .` / `-R .claude` for every jj command; don't `cd`.
- `vc-x1 push` wraps commit→bookmark→push→finalize with two approval gates (review, message).
- After `finalize`: hard stop the turn — no prose, no tools, wait for user.
- Pre-commit checklist: fmt, clippy, test, install, retest, update `todo.md` / `chores-*.md` / `README.md`.
- Version bump first; `X.Y.Z-N` for multi-step, `X.Y.Z` for single-step.
- Doc comments on every `.rs` file/fn/method; `// OK: …` on every non-test `unwrap*`.
- "The bot thinks …" prefix for speculation in durable text.

Ready for direction.

#
# workspace-path is this repo's path relative to the workspace root.
# Used to resolve changeID paths in git trailers (e.g. ochid: /changeID).
# other-repo is the relative path to the counterpart repo.

[workspace]
path = "/"
other-repo = ".claude"

content.content:
that has `.claude/` as a subdir (covers `LICENSE-*` etc. for
   both sides in one place). Updates to `vc-x1 init` / `clone`
   needed for the new layout.
1. Source-code design ref sweep + CLAUDE.md codification:
   adopt section-name + `blob/main/...` URL pattern for source
   code refs to designs; codify in CLAUDE.md alongside the
   existing markdown ref conventions. Sweep targets:
   src/push.rs lines 4, 121, 645, 1219. [68]
1. Richer bookmark enumeration: per-bookmark remote presence + tracking status [52]
1. Per-line/per-thread runtime log points (future, maybe) [36]

1. Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility

1. Add integration tests in tests/ for subcommands using temp jj repos (tempfile crate)

content.content:
51	## Generalize --scope to all commands (design)
52	
53	Surfaced 2026-04-23 (from the user, top of todo): vc-x1 commands
54	should support both single and dual repo modes. The technique
55	sketched for `vc-x1 push` (`--scope=app|claude|both`) is the
56	right shape, but the scope concept should be a project-wide
57	convention rather than push-only.
58	
59	**Naming:** `--scope=app|other|both` — "other" rather than
60	"claude" because the second repo isn't always `.claude`; could
61	be a different bot or a different sibling project structure.
62	
63	**Why scope-first** (before adding more commands or push
64	features): each new command that operates across both repos
65	would otherwise need its own ad-hoc handling. Locking in the
66	convention now means new commands inherit it for free, and
67	existing commands (sync, finalize, push) get retrofitted under
68	the same vocabulary.
69	
70	**Rough scope of the change:**
71	
72	- Shared CLI flag definition + parsing helper (likely in
73	  `common.rs`).
74	- `sync`: already accepts `-R` for single-repo. Migrate to
75	  `--scope`; alias or deprecate `-R` (TBD below).
76	- `push`: `--scope` per the existing design, with
77	  warn-on-WC-mismatch.
78	- `finalize`: already takes `--repo`; rationalize naming.
79	- New commands (`status` etc.) start with `--scope` from day 1.
80	
81	**Open:** do we deprecate `-R` immediately, alias it, or keep
82	both indefinitely? Probably: alias for one minor cycle,
83	deprecate, remove. Decide before implementation.
84	
85	## Push hardening: state + stage sanity (design)
86	
87	Two related correctness fixes for `vc-x1 push` resume behavior.
88	Both surfaced 2026-04-22 from a 0.37.1 dogfood incident and are
89	described together because they tackle the same failure mode
90	(silent false success after stale state).
91	
92	**The incident:** 0.37.1 push errored at finalize-claude;
93	out-of-band recovery (manual finalize + force-push of a squash)
94	moved the world forward; 0.37.2 push then resumed at the parked
95	finalize-claude, no-op'd, and falsely declared "completed all
96	stages" while working copies still held uncommitted changes.
97	
98	**State-sanity preflight on resume.** Before any stage runs,
99	verify saved state matches reality:
100	
101	- `state.app_chid` still exists (not abandoned/rewritten).
102	- `main` bookmark at `state.app_chid`'s commit.
103	- `state.claude_chid` consistent with `.claude` working copy.
104	
105	On mismatch, refuse with a loud "state is stale — run
106	`vc-x1 push --restart`" error.
107	
108	**Stage-prereq verification + honest completion.** Each stage
109	declares what it expects (working-copy dirty for commit-app;
110	bookmark at specific commit for bookmark-both; etc.); the
111	dispatcher checks before running. "Completed all stages" should
112	only print when stages genuinely ran or were
113	verified-already-done, not when they were skipped without
114	verification.
115	
116	**Implementation order:** state-sanity first (broader guard),
117	then per-stage prereqs (more invasive). Both should land before
118	the `--squash` work to avoid building on a known-fragile resume
119	path.
120	
121	## bm-track silent-when-clean (design)
122	
123	Today bm-track prints two lines per command (entry + exit)
124	unconditionally. In steady state both lines say
125	`tracked,tracked` — pure noise. Defer-flagged in 0.37.4 until
126	signal-confidence was established through more dogfood; several
127	0.37.x cycles have now used the probe with no false positives
128	or missed regressions.
129	
130	**Proposed behavior:**
131	
132	- Always probe on entry and exit (no change).
133	- Print on entry only when state isn't fully tracked.
134	- Print on exit when state isn't fully tracked OR when the exit
135	  state differs from entry.
136	- When printing on exit, include the entry state in the output
137	  so the transition is explicit:
138	  `bm-track vc-x1 <cmd>: enter=<state> → exit=<state>`.
139	
140	The entry-state-in-exit-output requirement is important —
141	"entry was ok (not printed), exit failed" is inference;
142	"entry=tracked, exit=NOT_TRACKED" in one line is evidence.
143	
144	**Implementation note:** record entry state in a local variable
145	at the enter call site and pass into the exit call.
146	
147	## Non-tracking-remote bookmark detection (design)
148	
149	Diagnosed 2026-04-22 dogfood: jj's tracking state is
150	**per-workspace** (local `.jj` store), not shared via git refs.
151	Sync across machines transfers refs but not the tracking flag —
152	so a fresh workspace fetched-into never auto-tracks. The failure
153	surfaces only when `jj git push` is attempted, which is too late
154	(push-app already succeeded in our case).
155	
156	**Policy (decided):** error loudly, with the exact
157	`jj bookmark track <b> --remote=<r> -R <repo>` remediation
158	command. No self-heal — keeps the fix explicit and visible.
159	
160	**Scope (every command that creates or mutates repo state):**
161	
162	- `vc-x1 init`: already correct (Step 10 calls `jj bookmark
163	  track`). Add the check as a post-condition sanity assertion
164	  anyway.
165	- `vc-x1 clone`: does `git clone` then `jj git init --colocate`.
166	  Whether that combination auto-establishes tracking in jj's
167	  workspace store is unclear; probably needs an explicit
168	  `jj bookmark track` for each cloned bookmark after the init.
169	  Verify and fix.
170	- `vc-x1 test-fixture`: Step 7's `jj git push --bookmark main`
171	  establishes tracking as a side effect of the first push.
172	  Works correctly — confirmed via `jj bookmark list --tracked`
173	  on a fresh fixture. No change needed; the post-condition
174	  sanity check would naturally cover it.
175	- `vc-x1 sync` preflight: detect + error.
176	- `vc-x1 push` preflight: detect + error (before any mutation).
177	- `vc-x1 finalize`: detect + error before the squash, so a
178	  failed push doesn't leave a half-finalized state.
179	
180	**Shared helper:** `common::verify_tracking(repo, bookmark,
181	remote) -> Result<(), Err>` or similar. Probably use
182	`jj bookmark list --tracked -T <template>` under the hood
183	rather than parsing human-readable output.
184	
185	## Scope design refinements (0.37.8)

## Generalize --scope to all commands (design)

Surfaced 2026-04-23 (from the user, top of todo): vc-x1 commands
should support both single and dual repo modes. The technique
sketched for `vc-x1 push` (`--scope=app|claude|both`) is the
right shape, but the scope concept should be a project-wide
convention rather than push-only.

**Naming:** `--scope=app|other|both` — "other" rather than
"claude" because the second repo isn't always `.claude`; could
be a different bot or a different sibling project structure.

**Why scope-first** (before adding more commands or push
features): each new command that operates across both repos
would otherwise need its own ad-hoc handling. Locking in the
convention now means new commands inherit it for free, and
existing commands (sync, finalize, push) get retrofitted under
the same vocabulary.

**Rough scope of the change:**

- Shared CLI flag definition + parsing helper (likely in
  `common.rs`).
- `sync`: already accepts `-R` for single-repo. Migrate to
  `--scope`; alias or deprecate `-R` (TBD below).
- `push`: `--scope` per the existing design, with
  warn-on-WC-mismatch.
- `finalize`: already takes `--repo`; rationalize naming.
- New commands (`status` etc.) start with `--scope` from day 1.

**Open:** do we deprecate `-R` immediately, alias it, or keep
both indefinitely? Probably: alias for one minor cycle,
deprecate, remove. Decide before implementation.

## Push hardening: state + stage sanity (design)

Two related correctness fixes for `vc-x1 push` resume behavior.
Both surfaced 2026-04-22 from a 0.37.1 dogfood incident and are
described together because they tackle the same failure mode
(silent false success after stale state).

**The incident:** 0.37.1 push errored at finalize-claude;
out-of-band recovery (manual finalize + force-push of a squash)
moved the world forward; 0.37.2 push then resumed at the parked
finalize-claude, no-op'd, and falsely declared "completed all
stages" while working copies still held uncommitted changes.

**State-sanity preflight on resume.** Before any stage runs,
verify saved state matches reality:

- `state.app_chid` still exists (not abandoned/rewritten).
- `main` bookmark at `state.app_chid`'s commit.
- `state.claude_chid` consistent with `.claude` working copy.

On mismatch, refuse with a loud "state is stale — run
`vc-x1 push --restart`" error.

**Stage-prereq verification + honest completion.** Each stage
declares what it expects (working-copy dirty for commit-app;
bookmark at specific commit for bookmark-both; etc.); the
dispatcher checks before running. "Completed all stages" should
only print when stages genuinely ran or were
verified-already-done, not when they were skipped without
verification.

**Implementation order:** state-sanity first (broader guard),
then per-stage prereqs (more invasive). Both should land before
the `--squash` work to avoid building on a known-fragile resume
path.

## bm-track silent-when-clean (design)

Today bm-track prints two lines per command (entry + exit)
unconditionally. In steady state both lines say
`tracked,tracked` — pure noise. Defer-flagged in 0.37.4 until
signal-confidence was established through more dogfood; several
0.37.x cycles have now used the probe with no false positives
or missed regressions.

**Proposed behavior:**

- Always probe on entry and exit (no change).
- Print on entry only when state isn't fully tracked.
- Print on exit when state isn't fully tracked OR when the exit
  state differs from entry.
- When printing on exit, include the entry state in the output
  so the transition is explicit:
  `bm-track vc-x1 <cmd>: enter=<state> → exit=<state>`.

The entry-state-in-exit-output requirement is important —
"entry was ok (not printed), exit failed" is inference;
"entry=tracked, exit=NOT_TRACKED" in one line is evidence.

**Implementation note:** record entry state in a local variable
at the enter call site and pass into the exit call.

## Non-tracking-remote bookmark detection (design)

Diagnosed 2026-04-22 dogfood: jj's tracking state is
**per-workspace** (local `.jj` store), not shared via git refs.
Sync across machines transfers refs but not the tracking flag —
so a fresh workspace fetched-into never auto-tracks. The failure
surfaces only when `jj git push` is attempted, which is too late
(push-app already succeeded in our case).

**Policy (decided):** error loudly, with the exact
`jj bookmark track <b> --remote=<r> -R <repo>` remediation
command. No self-heal — keeps the fix explicit and visible.

**Scope (every command that creates or mutates repo state):**

- `vc-x1 init`: already correct (Step 10 calls `jj bookmark
  track`). Add the check as a post-condition sanity assertion
  anyway.
- `vc-x1 clone`: does `git clone` then `jj git init --colocate`.
  Whether that combination auto-establishes tracking in jj's
  workspace store is unclear; probably needs an explicit
  `jj bookmark track` for each cloned bookmark after the init.
  Verify and fix.
- `vc-x1 test-fixture`: Step 7's `jj git push --bookmark main`
  establishes tracking as a side effect of the first push.
  Works correctly — confirmed via `jj bookmark list --tracked`
  on a fresh fixture. No change needed; the post-condition
  sanity check would naturally cover it.
- `vc-x1 sync` preflight: detect + error.
- `vc-x1 push` preflight: detect + error (before any mutation).
- `vc-x1 finalize`: detect + error before the squash, so a
  failed push doesn't leave a half-finalized state.

**Shared helper:** `common::verify_tracking(repo, bookmark,
remote) -> Result<(), Err>` or similar. Probably use
`jj bookmark list --tracked -T <template>` under the hood
rather than parsing human-readable output.

## Scope design refinements (0.37.8)

content.content:
185	## Scope design refinements (0.37.8)
186	
187	Notes-only release. Captures six refinements to the scope and
188	squash designs ([57], [60]) from a 2026-04-23 dogfood discussion
189	(the manual squash workflow used to fold late changes into the
190	0.37.7 commit, plus follow-up Q&A on cwd independence and
191	plain-old-repo handling).
192	
193	**Scope semantic invariant.** With `--scope=app|other|both`
194	(per [60]):
195	
196	- `--scope=both` (or omitted; `both` is the default) → dual-repo
197	  operation.
198	- `--scope=app` → single-repo operation on the app repo (direct
199	  reference).
200	- `--scope=other` → single-repo operation on the "other" repo
201	  (indirect reference; resolves via `.vc-config.toml`'s
202	  `[workspace].other-repo` field, which already exists).
203	
204	Anything that isn't `both` is single-repo. The dispatcher branches
205	once on this invariant; new commands inheriting `--scope` get the
206	semantic for free.
207	
208	**`--other` is project config, not a per-command flag.** Came up
209	during the same discussion: should there be a `--other=PATH` flag
210	to override the project's "other" repo per command? Answer: no.
211	That belongs in `.vc-config.toml` (already there as
212	`[workspace].other-repo`). Keeping `--other` out of the
213	per-command surface keeps the flag set small and the per-command
214	vocabulary uniform across all commands — "other" always resolves
215	the same way.
216	
217	**Cwd independence + counterpart-rename suggestion.** Scope names
218	are workspace-anchored, not cwd-relative. Each repo has its own
219	`.vc-config.toml` self-describing its role:
220	
221	- App's config: `[workspace] path = "/" other-repo = ".claude"`.
222	- `.claude`'s config: `[workspace] path = "/.claude"
223	  other-repo = ".."`.
224	
225	`path = "/"` identifies the app repo (only the app has it). The
226	dispatcher reads the local config, determines "am I app or other?"
227	via `path`, then resolves `--scope=app` and `--scope=other` to the
228	right physical paths regardless of cwd. From `cd .claude`:
229	
230	- `--scope=app` → `..` (the app repo, found via `.claude`'s
231	  `other-repo`).
232	- `--scope=other` → `.` (`.claude` itself, since `path != "/"`
233	  → I AM the workspace's "other").
234	
235	Naming gotcha: the config field `other-repo` means "this repo's
236	counterpart" (cwd-flavored direction; from `.claude` it's `..` =
237	the app), while CLI `--scope=other` means "the workspace's 'other'
238	role" (workspace-anchored; always = `.claude`). They coincide
239	from app's cwd (both physically = `.claude`) but diverge from
240	`.claude`'s cwd (config `other-repo` = `..`, CLI `--scope=other`
241	= `.`). Consider renaming the config field to `counterpart` or
242	`peer` to disambiguate from the CLI scope name.
243	
244	**Plain-old-repo handling: three workspace states.** vc-x1 should
245	be practical without a full workspace too. Three distinguishable
246	states, all described by `.vc-config.toml` presence/contents:
247	
248	- **POR** — no `.vc-config.toml`. Pure git/jj repo, vc-x1
249	  doesn't know it exists. Implicit `--scope=app`;
250	  `--scope=other/both` errors with "not in a vc-x1 workspace
251	  (no `.vc-config.toml`) — drop --scope or use --scope=app".
252	- **Single-repo workspace** — `.vc-config.toml` with `path`
253	  only (no `other-repo`). vc-x1-aware, no companion. Example:
254	  `vc-template-x1`. Implicit `--scope=app`; `--scope=other/both`
255	  errors with "no other-repo configured. Add `other-repo = …`
256	  to `.vc-config.toml` to enable dual-repo operations".
257	- **Dual-repo workspace** — `.vc-config.toml` with `path` +
258	  `other-repo`. Full setup. Example: `vc-x1` itself.
259	  `--scope=app/other/both` all valid.
260	
261	Edge cases: a `.vc-config.toml` with `path != "/"` and no
262	`other-repo` (e.g. `path = "/.claude"`) — the repo identifies as
263	the "other" side but its companion is missing. Error: "config
264	identifies this as `/.claude` but no `other-repo` to point at the
265	app side; companion is missing." Dual-repo with `other-repo`
266	pointing somewhere that doesn't exist → loud error (workspace
267	state corrupted), not POR fallback.
268	
269	**`vc-x1 push --squash` composition.** With `--scope` in place,
270	the squash design (per [57]) composes naturally:
271	
272	- `vc-x1 push --squash` (no scope) → squash both repos and
273	  force-push both. End-to-end version of the manual two-step
274	  recipe in CLAUDE.md "Late changes after push".
275	- `vc-x1 push --squash --scope=app` → squash app only.

file.content:
## Scope design refinements (0.37.8)

Notes-only release. Captures six refinements to the scope and
squash designs ([57], [60]) from a 2026-04-23 dogfood discussion
(the manual squash workflow used to fold late changes into the
0.37.7 commit, plus follow-up Q&A on cwd independence and
plain-old-repo handling).

**Scope semantic invariant.** With `--scope=app|other|both`
(per [60]):

- `--scope=both` (or omitted; `both` is the default) → dual-repo
  operation.
- `--scope=app` → single-repo operation on the app repo (direct
  reference).
- `--scope=other` → single-repo operation on the "other" repo
  (indirect reference; resolves via `.vc-config.toml`'s
  `[workspace].other-repo` field, which already exists).

Anything that isn't `both` is single-repo. The dispatcher branches
once on this invariant; new commands inheriting `--scope` get the
semantic for free.

**`--other` is project config, not a per-command flag.** Came up
during the same discussion: should there be a `--other=PATH` flag
to override the project's "other" repo per command? Answer: no.
That belongs in `.vc-config.toml` (already there as
`[workspace].other-repo`). Keeping `--other` out of the
per-command surface keeps the flag set small and the per-command
vocabulary uniform across all commands — "other" always resolves
the same way.

**Cwd independence + counterpart-rename suggestion.** Scope names
are workspace-anchored, not cwd-relative. Each repo has its own
`.vc-config.toml` self-describing its role:

- App's config: `[workspace] path = "/" other-repo = ".claude"`.
- `.claude`'s config: `[workspace] path = "/.claude"
  other-repo = ".."`.

`path = "/"` identifies the app repo (only the app has it). The
dispatcher reads the local config, determines "am I app or other?"
via `path`, then resolves `--scope=app` and `--scope=other` to the
right physical paths regardless of cwd. From `cd .claude`:

- `--scope=app` → `..` (the app repo, found via `.claude`'s
  `other-repo`).
- `--scope=other` → `.` (`.claude` itself, since `path != "/"`
  → I AM the workspace's "other").

Naming gotcha: the config field `other-repo` means "this repo's
counterpart" (cwd-flavored direction; from `.claude` it's `..` =
the app), while CLI `--scope=other` means "the workspace's 'other'
role" (workspace-anchored; always = `.claude`). They coincide
from app's cwd (both physically = `.claude`) but diverge from
`.claude`'s cwd (config `other-repo` = `..`, CLI `--scope=other`
= `.`). Consider renaming the config field to `counterpart` or
`peer` to disambiguate from the CLI scope name.

**Plain-old-repo handling: three workspace states.** vc-x1 should
be practical without a full workspace too. Three distinguishable
states, all described by `.vc-config.toml` presence/contents:

- **POR** — no `.vc-config.toml`. Pure git/jj repo, vc-x1
  doesn't know it exists. Implicit `--scope=app`;
  `--scope=other/both` errors with "not in a vc-x1 workspace
  (no `.vc-config.toml`) — drop --scope or use --scope=app".
- **Single-repo workspace** — `.vc-config.toml` with `path`
  only (no `other-repo`). vc-x1-aware, no companion. Example:
  `vc-template-x1`. Implicit `--scope=app`; `--scope=other/both`
  errors with "no other-repo configured. Add `other-repo = …`
  to `.vc-config.toml` to enable dual-repo operations".
- **Dual-repo workspace** — `.vc-config.toml` with `path` +
  `other-repo`. Full setup. Example: `vc-x1` itself.
  `--scope=app/other/both` all valid.

Edge cases: a `.vc-config.toml` with `path != "/"` and no
`other-repo` (e.g. `path = "/.claude"`) — the repo identifies as
the "other" side but its companion is missing. Error: "config
identifies this as `/.claude` but no `other-repo` to point at the
app side; companion is missing." Dual-repo with `other-repo`
pointing somewhere that doesn't exist → loud error (workspace
state corrupted), not POR fallback.

**`vc-x1 push --squash` composition.** With `--scope` in place,
the squash design (per [57]) composes naturally:

- `vc-x1 push --squash` (no scope) → squash both repos and
  force-push both. End-to-end version of the manual two-step
  recipe in CLAUDE.md "Late changes after push".
- `vc-x1 push --squash --scope=app` → squash app only.
- `vc-x1 push --squash --scope=other` → squash other only.

**Dogfood validation (2026-04-23).** The manual recipe was applied
successfully to both repos in sequence to fold late changes into
the 0.37.7 commit. Observations worth carrying into the `--squash`
implementation:

- jj's `jj squash --ignore-immutable` preserves the changeID of
  the squashed-into commit. Both repos' ochid trailers stayed
  valid through the squash without any fixup. The future
  `--squash` implementation can rely on this — no ochid rewrite
  needed.
- The trimmed "Late changes after push" recipe (0.37.6, two
  lines for the `@-` case) worked identically on both sides;
  no `jj bookmark set` needed since the bookmark moves with the
  rewritten commit.
- Push state was already cleared at the end of the prior
  successful push (state reported "completed all stages (state
  cleared)"). The post-recovery `--restart` / `rm` step
  documented in CLAUDE.md "Manual finalize fallback" wasn't
  needed in this case — but the doc remains correct for cases
  where prior push didn't clean up.

- `notes/chores-06.md` — this subsection.
- `notes/todo.md` — Done entry + new `[65]` reference + new
  Todo entry for the template restructure (vc-template-x1 +
  `.claude/` subdir, captured separately for later
  implementation).

## Bookmark tracking verification (0.38.0)

Cross-command tracking verification per the [63] design — every
repo-modifying command checks its target bookmark for non-tracking
remote refs and errors with the exact remediation command if any

content.content:
1	use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};
2	use std::process::Command;
3	
4	use clap::Args;
5	use log::{LevelFilter, debug, info, warn};
6	
7	use crate::common::run;
8	
9	/// Fetch and sync a set of repos to their remotes.
10	///
11	/// Default is `--check`: fetch + classify + report, **error** if any
12	/// repo is `behind` or `diverged`. Pass `--no-check` to actually
13	/// rebase / fast-forward. On any failure the starting state of every
14	/// repo is restored via `jj op restore`.
15	///
16	/// Scripts and automation should pass `--check` or `--no-check`
17	/// explicitly rather than relying on the default — defaults can shift,
18	/// explicit flags lock in the contract.
19	///
20	/// Repo set defaults to the dual-repo workspace pair (`.` and
21	/// `.claude`); override with `-R` / `--repo` for single-repo projects
22	/// (e.g. `vc-template-x1`) or arbitrary multi-repo workspaces.
23	#[derive(Args, Debug)]
24	pub struct SyncArgs {
25	    /// Verify only — fetch + classify; error if any repo needs action.
26	    /// This is the default; pass explicitly in scripts.
27	    #[arg(long, conflicts_with = "no_check")]
28	    pub check: bool,
29	
30	    /// Apply — fetch + classify, then rebase / fast-forward as needed.
31	    #[arg(long, conflicts_with = "check")]
32	    pub no_check: bool,
33	
34	    /// Suppress all informational output (exit code signals result)
35	    #[arg(short, long)]
36	    pub quiet: bool,
37	
38	    /// Bookmark to sync in each repo
39	    #[arg(long, default_value = "main")]
40	    pub bookmark: String,
41	
42	    /// Remote to sync against
43	    #[arg(long, default_value = "origin")]
44	    pub remote: String,
45	
46	    /// Path to jj repo; repeatable or comma-separated [default: `.,.claude`]
47	    #[arg(short = 'R', long = "repo", value_name = "PATH")]
48	    pub repos: Vec<PathBuf>,
49	}
50	
51	/// Default repo set when `-R` isn't given — the canonical dual-repo pair.
52	const DEFAULT_REPOS: [&str; 2] = [".", ".claude"];
53	
54	/// Resolve the caller's `-R` flags into a concrete repo list.
55	///
56	/// Empty input (no `-R` given) falls back to `DEFAULT_REPOS`. Each
57	/// provided value is also split on `,` and trimmed, so
58	/// `-R .,.claude` works identically to `-R . -R .claude`.
59	fn resolve_repos(raw: &[PathBuf]) -> Vec<PathBuf> {
60	    if raw.is_empty() {
61	        return DEFAULT_REPOS.iter().map(PathBuf::from).collect();
62	    }
63	    raw.iter()
64	        .flat_map(|p| {
65	            p.to_string_lossy()
66	                .split(',')
67	                .map(|s| PathBuf::from(s.trim()))
68	                .collect::<Vec<_>>()
69	        })
70	        .collect()
71	}
72	
73	/// Relationship between a local bookmark and its remote counterpart.
74	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
75	pub enum State {
76	    /// Local and remote point at the same commit.
77	    UpToDate,
78	    /// Local is a strict ancestor of remote — fast-forward possible.
79	    Behind { local: String, remote: String },
80	    /// Remote is a strict ancestor of local — nothing to pull in.
81	    Ahead { local: String, remote: String },
82	    /// Neither is an ancestor of the other — needs rebase.
83	    Diverged { local: String, remote: String },
84	    /// The bookmark has no `@<remote>` counterpart.
85	    NoRemote,
86	}
87	
88	/// Per-repo context accumulated between the snapshot and action phases.
89	#[derive(Debug)]
90	struct RepoCtx {
91	    path: PathBuf,
92	    #[allow(dead_code)]
93	    op_id: String,
94	    state: State,
95	}
96	
97	/// CLI entry point for the `sync` subcommand.
98	///
99	/// Thin wrapper over `sync_repos` that resolves the `-R` flag into a
100	/// concrete repo list (falling back to the dual-repo default) and
101	/// forwards the rest of the args. Tests call `sync_repos` directly
102	/// with absolute fixture paths.
103	///
104	/// When `--quiet` is set, the global log filter is temporarily clamped
105	/// to `Warn` for the duration of the call and restored on return, so
106	/// `info!` calls throughout sync (plus any subprocess-stderr routed
107	/// through `common::run`) go dark. Errors still surface at `Warn` /
108	/// `Error` so script callers don't lose diagnostics.
109	pub fn sync(args: &SyncArgs) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
110	    let repos = resolve_repos(&args.repos);
111	    if args.quiet {
112	        let prev = log::max_level();
113	        log::set_max_level(LevelFilter::Warn);
114	        let result = sync_repos(&repos, args);
115	        log::set_max_level(prev);
116	        result
117	    } else {
118	        sync_repos(&repos, args)
119	    }
120	}

content.text:
I've read the design (chores-06 `[60]` + `[65]`), the workspace-state invariant (three states: POR / single-repo / dual-repo), the existing `-R`/`--repo`/`--scope`-shaped surfaces, and how each command currently assumes dual-repo today. Here's what I'd propose — I want your approval on shape + open questions before writing any code.

## Current assumptions to dismantle

- `sync`: `DEFAULT_REPOS = [".", ".claude"]` hard-coded in `sync.rs:52`.
- `push`: always dual; `finalize-claude` stage hard-codes `.claude` path in `push.rs:1202/1207/1213`.
- `finalize`: already single-repo per invocation (`--repo`), so it's fine semantically; naming needs rationalization.
- `main.rs bm_track`: already walks up for `.vc-config.toml` with `path="/"` — most of the workspace-root detection is there but limited to dual-repo assumption (`repos` array hard-codes `.claude`).
- `.vc-config.toml`: already has `path = "/"` and `other-repo = ".claude"` — no schema change needed for this cycle.

## Proposed cycle: `0.40.0-N`, five steps

Multi-step, not single-step — this is foundational and exploratory (scope-resolution semantics deserve their own commits; retrofits per command are each discretely testable).

- **0.40.0-0** — foundation, no behavior change. Add `common::workspace` module: `WorkspaceState` enum (`Por`, `SingleRepo{root}`, `DualRepo{root, other}`), `detect()` walker, `ScopeArg` flag type (`#[derive(Args)]`, values `app|other|both`), and `resolve(state, scope) → Vec<PathBuf>` enforcing the three-state policy per `[65]`. Unit tests. Move `main::find_workspace_root` into the new module.
- **0.40.0-1** — retrofit `sync`. Add `--scope`; default becomes "resolve via workspace state" (POR/single → `[.]`, dual → `[., other]`). Keep `-R` working unchanged (no deprecation this cycle). Mutual-exclude `--scope` and `-R` via clap.
- **0.40.0-2** — retrofit `push`. Add `--scope`. Use `workspace.other` instead of literal `.claude`. `--scope=app` skips `commit-claude` + `finalize-claude` stages. Refuse `other`/`both` outside dual. Update state file shape only if necessary (prefer additive).
- **0.40.0-3** — retrofit `finalize`. Keep `--repo` as the primary path input (it already works everywhere); add `--scope` as an alternative that resolves via workspace state. Update `main::bm_track` to use `WorkspaceState` instead of its hard-coded `.claude` probe.
- **0.40.0** — release: `notes/todo.md` Done, `notes/chores-06.md` subsection, CLAUDE.md refresh if new vocabulary surfaces, README updates for the three affected commands.

## Deliberately out of scope for this cycle

- `other-repo` → `counterpart` config rename ([65] suggestion). Add as a follow-up todo.
- Removing/deprecating `-R`. Design said "alias for one minor cycle, deprecate, remove." This cycle = the alias window; deprecation warning lands in 0.41.0.
- Retrofitting read-only commands (chid/desc/list/show/validate-desc/fix-desc). They already take `-R` and are multi-repo-flexible. A `--scope` convenience can be added later; not required for the "operate on any repo" goal.

## Open questions

1. **Scope default when `--scope` omitted on retrofitted commands:** I assumed "workspace-state driven" (POR/single → app, dual → both). Alternative is "always both, error if not dual" — more explicit but noisier in single-repo workspaces. Prefer state-driven?
2. **`-R` + `--scope` together:** clap mutual-exclusion (error) or silent precedence (one wins)? I lean error — catches confusion early.
3. **Single-step vs multi-step:** proposing multi-step `0.40.0-N`. Switch to single-step `0.40.0` if you'd rather one larger commit?

Let me know and I'll start with step 0.

content: Goal is generalizing `vc-x1` with `--scope=app|other|both` so it works on single-repo, dual-repo, or plain workspaces. I've proposed a 5-step `0.40.0-N` cycle starting with foundation work and am waiting for approval plus answers to three open questions before coding. (disable recaps in /config)

message.content:
1. if no scope is given:
 - And there is no .vc-config.toml assume --scope=app
 - And there is a .vc-config.toml but other-repo is missing or empty assume --scope=app
 - else assume --scope=both
2. If there is ambiguity in options/flags/parameters it is a fatal error
3. Multi-step:
 - -0 is the plan
 - -1 is changing vc-x1 init to handle --scope
 - All other commands it's an error
 - new functions can be in common.rs or init.rs or scope_helpers.rs not critical we can always refactor
 - The "plan" is always subject to change as we learn

content.text:
Good. I've confirmed `init.rs` writes `.vc-config.toml` with both `path` and `other-repo` today, creates the `.claude` subdir, and creates two GitHub repos — so step `-1` has a concrete `--scope=app` variant to build.

Here's the proposed chores-06 subsection for step `-0`. Nothing else changes in `-0` besides `Cargo.toml` bump + `todo.md` Todo→In Progress move.

---

**Proposed `notes/chores-06.md` new section (at end of file):**

```markdown
## Generalize --scope across commands (0.40.0)

Foundation for item #1 on todo ([60]): commands should support
single-repo, dual-repo, and plain-old-repo (POR) workspaces
without hard-coding the `.claude` dual-repo assumption.

**Scope default resolution** (no `--scope` given):

- No `.vc-config.toml` → `--scope=app`.
- `.vc-config.toml` present but `other-repo` missing or empty
  → `--scope=app`.
- `.vc-config.toml` with a non-empty `other-repo` → `--scope=both`.

**Ambiguity rule.** Any ambiguous combination of scope-related
flags on a single invocation (e.g. `-R` with `--scope`) is a
fatal error — callers must pick one way to express repo
selection per call.

**Cycle steps.**

- **0.40.0-0** — this plan. Notes + version bump only.
- **0.40.0-1** — `vc-x1 init --scope=app|other|both`.
  - `app` → single-repo workspace (`.vc-config.toml` with
    `path = "/"` and no `other-repo`; no `.claude` subdir; one
    GitHub repo; no `ochid:` trailer on the initial commit).
  - `both` (default) → current dual-repo behavior.
  - `other` → error; meaningless at init time.
  - Every other subcommand errors loudly if `--scope` is passed.
- Subsequent `-N` steps wire `--scope` into sync, push,
  finalize, and the read-only commands. Specifics decided when
  each lands.

**Helper placement is deliberately open** — `common.rs`, `init.rs`,
or a dedicated `scope.rs` / `scope_helpers.rs` are all fine for
step `-1`; we can refactor later once more call sites exist. The
plan is subject to change as we learn; `-0` records the current
best guess, not a contract.
```

**Plus `notes/todo.md`:** move the `--scope` generalization item from `## Todo` to `## In Progress`, and add a new reference `[70]: /notes/chores-06.md#generalize---scope-across-commands-0400`.

**Plus `Cargo.toml`:** `version = "0.40.0-0"`.

## Step `-0` file list

1. `Cargo.toml` — version bump.
2. `Cargo.lock` — regenerates on `cargo check`.
3. `notes/chores-06.md` — append the section above.
4. `notes/todo.md` — In Progress move + ref `[70]`.

No code, no tests, no README. Pre-commit checklist is shortened: `cargo fmt` and `cargo check` (fmt touches nothing meaningful here; check confirms `Cargo.lock` regenerates cleanly). No clippy/test delta expected since no `.rs` changes.

OK to proceed with these edits?

# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress



## Todo

A markdown list of tasks to do in the near future, ordered
highest-priority first. Keep entries brief — 1-3 lines.
Detailed motivation, safety requirements, and ordering belong
in `notes/chores-NN.md` design subsections; link via `[N]` ref.

Items use lazy numbering — every entry begins with `1. `; the
markdown renderer auto-numbers them, so reorder/insert without
renumbering. Reference by displayed number ("let's work on #3").

1. vc-x1 commands: support single and dual repo. Generalize via
   `--scope=app|other|both` across all commands; foundational
   for new commands and for retrofitting sync / push / finalize
   under one vocabulary. [60]
1. vc-x1 push: `--scope=app|claude|both` flag. Applies the
   generalized convention; warn on scope/WC mismatch. [57],[60]
1. vc-x1 push: `--squash` flag. Squashes WC into `@-` via
   `--ignore-immutable` and force-pushes; needs
   `--force-with-lease`-equivalent + state-sanity preflight in
   place first. [57]
1. vc-x1 push: `--message-file PATH` flag. Git-style commit
   message file (first line = title, blank, rest = body).
   Alternative to `--title` + `--body`. [58]
1. Mirror `--check` / `--no-check` onto `vc-x1 push` (forwards
   through to the preflight `vc-x1 sync` invocation).
   0.37.1 hard-codes `--check`; default stays `--check`.
1. Add `status` (alias `st`) subcommand: `jj st` across both
   repos in one shot. Uses `--scope` from day one. Natural
   home for the working-copy signal called out in [54].
1. Add `validate-repo` subcommand: diagnostic that runs all

# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress



## Todo

A markdown list of tasks to do in the near future, ordered
highest-priority first. Keep entries brief — 1-3 lines.
Detailed motivation, safety requirements, and ordering belong
in `notes/chores-NN.md` design subsections; link via `[N]` ref.

Items use lazy numbering — every entry begins with `1. `; the
markdown renderer auto-numbers them, so reorder/insert without
renumbering. Reference by displayed number ("let's work on #3").

1. vc-x1 commands: support single and dual repo. Generalize via
   `--scope=app|other|both` across all commands; foundational
   for new commands and for retrofitting sync / push / finalize
   under one vocabulary. [60]
1. vc-x1 push: `--scope=app|claude|both` flag. Applies the
   generalized convention; warn on scope/WC mismatch. [57],[60]
1. vc-x1 push: `--squash` flag. Squashes WC into `@-` via
   `--ignore-immutable` and force-pushes; needs
   `--force-with-lease`-equivalent + state-sanity preflight in
   place first. [57]
1. vc-x1 push: `--message-file PATH` flag. Git-style commit
   message file (first line = title, blank, rest = body).
   Alternative to `--title` + `--body`. [58]
1. Mirror `--check` / `--no-check` onto `vc-x1 push` (forwards
   through to the preflight `vc-x1 sync` invocation).
   0.37.1 hard-codes `--check`; default stays `--check`.
1. Add `status` (alias `st`) subcommand: `jj st` across both
   repos in one shot. Uses `--scope` from day one. Natural
   home for the working-copy signal called out in [54].
1. Add `validate-repo` subcommand: diagnostic that runs all
   `verify_*` checks (tracking, push state freshness, ochid
   integrity, conflicts, config sanity, working-copy state)
   and reports per-check pass/fail. Exit code = number of
   failed checks. Implementation: promote
   `verify_state_sanity` / `verify_completion_sanity` from
   push.rs to `common.rs`. [69]
1. sync: surface working-copy state in the up-to-date summary
   (per-repo pending-files count or compact stat). Wording-only
   fix shipped in 0.37.1; this is the design+impl. [54]
1. bm-track silent-when-clean refinement. Print on entry/exit
   only when state isn't fully tracked or when exit state
   differs from entry. [62]
1. "Oh shit" revert — post-success undo via `.vc-x1-ops/`
   anchor dir. Idea-stage; every repo-mutating command drops a
   pre-op snapshot, `vc-x1 undo` restores both repos. [57]
1. vc-x1 test-fixture should refuse `--path` values that resolve
   inside the current workspace root (error or warn). Dogfood
   surfaced this: `--path ./tf-1` inside the repo let jj
   snapshot the fixture's bare-git remotes — 56-file noise blob.
1. Restructure templates: replace separate `vc-template-x1` +
   `vc-template-x1.claude` repos with a single `vc-template-x1`
   that has `.claude/` as a subdir (covers `LICENSE-*` etc. for
   both sides in one place). Updates to `vc-x1 init` / `clone`
   needed for the new layout.
1. Source-code design ref sweep + CLAUDE.md codification:
   adopt section-name + `blob/main/...` URL pattern for source
   code refs to designs; codify in CLAUDE.md alongside the
   existing markdown ref conventions. Sweep targets:
   src/push.rs lines 4, 121, 645, 1219. [68]
1. Richer bookmark enumeration: per-bookmark remote presence + tracking status [52]
1. Per-line/per-thread runtime log points (future, maybe) [36]
1. Add Windows symlink support via `std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir` [37]
1. Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility
1. Add -p, --parents, -c, --children so parent and child counts can be asymmetric
1. Add integration tests in tests/ for subcommands using temp jj repos (tempfile crate)
1. Fix .claude repo history: dev0 through dev2 sessions squashed into wrong commit [4],[5]
1. Add `vc-x1 setup` subcommand: completions install, .claude repo init, symlink setup [27]
1. Add dynamic revision completion via `ArgValueCompleter` (jj doesn't complete revsets either) [28],[29]

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

- CLAUDE.md refresh + memory migration (0.36.1) [49]
- Lift sync's inline test harness into shared `test_helpers` module (0.36.2) [51]
- Sync improvements: -R flag + quieter dry-run + sync-before-work discipline (0.36.3) [50]
- push subcommand scaffolding: flag surface, Stage enum, stub (0.37.0-0) [48]
- push state machine: state file, --status/--restart/--from, stage stubs (0.37.0-1) [48]
- push real stage bodies + jj-op snapshot rollback (0.37.0-2) [48]
- push integration tests + workspace-root refactor (0.37.0-3) [48]
- push interactivity: review prompt, $EDITOR, message persistence (0.37.0-4) [48]

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress

1. vc-x1 commands: support single and dual repo. Generalize via
   `--scope=app|other|both` across all commands; foundational
   for new commands and for retrofitting sync / push / finalize
   under one vocabulary. [60],[70]

## Todo

A markdown list of tasks to do in the near future, ordered
highest-priority first. Keep entries brief — 1-3 lines.
Detailed motivation, safety requirements, and ordering belong
in `notes/chores-NN.md` design subsections; link via `[N]` ref.

Items use lazy numbering — every entry begins with `1. `; the
markdown renderer auto-numbers them, so reorder/insert without
renumbering. Reference by displayed number ("let's work on #3").
1. vc-x1 push: `--scope=app|claude|both` flag. Applies the
   generalized convention; warn on scope/WC mismatch. [57],[60]
1. vc-x1 push: `--squash` flag. Squashes WC into `@-` via
   `--ignore-immutable` and force-pushes; needs
   `--force-with-lease`-equivalent + state-sanity preflight in
   place first. [57]
1. vc-x1 push: `--message-file PATH` flag. Git-style commit
   message file (first line = title, blank, rest = body).
   Alternative to `--title` + `--body`. [58]
1. Mirror `--check` / `--no-check` onto `vc-x1 push` (forwards
   through to the preflight `vc-x1 sync` invocation).
   0.37.1 hard-codes `--check`; default stays `--check`.
1. Add `status` (alias `st`) subcommand: `jj st` across both
   repos in one shot. Uses `--scope` from day one. Natural
   home for the working-copy signal called out in [54].
1. Add `validate-repo` subcommand: diagnostic that runs all
   `verify_*` checks (tracking, push state freshness, ochid
   integrity, conflicts, config sanity, working-copy state)
   and reports per-check pass/fail. Exit code = number of
   failed checks. Implementation: promote
   `verify_state_sanity` / `verify_completion_sanity` from
   push.rs to `common.rs`. [69]
1. sync: surface working-copy state in the up-to-date summary
   (per-repo pending-files count or compact stat). Wording-only
   fix shipped in 0.37.1; this is the design+impl. [54]
1. bm-track silent-when-clean refinement. Print on entry/exit
   only when state isn't fully tracked or when exit state
   differs from entry. [62]
1. "Oh shit" revert — post-success undo via `.vc-x1-ops/`
   anchor dir. Idea-stage; every repo-mutating command drops a
   pre-op snapshot, `vc-x1 undo` restores both repos. [57]
1. vc-x1 test-fixture should refuse `--path` values that resolve
   inside the current workspace root (error or warn). Dogfood
   surfaced this: `--path ./tf-1` inside the repo let jj
   snapshot the fixture's bare-git remotes — 56-file noise blob.
1. Restructure templates: replace separate `vc-template-x1` +
   `vc-template-x1.claude` repos with a single `vc-template-x1`
   that has `.claude/` as a subdir (covers `LICENSE-*` etc. for
   both sides in one place). Updates to `vc-x1 init` / `clone`
   needed for the new layout.
1. Source-code design ref sweep + CLAUDE.md codification:
   adopt section-name + `blob/main/...` URL pattern for source
   code refs to designs; codify in CLAUDE.md alongside the
   existing markdown ref conventions. Sweep targets:
   src/push.rs lines 4, 121, 645, 1219. [68]
1. Richer bookmark enumeration: per-bookmark remote presence + tracking status [52]
1. Per-line/per-thread runtime log points (future, maybe) [36]
1. Add Windows symlink support via `std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir` [37]
1. Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility
1. Add -p, --parents, -c, --children so parent and child counts can be asymmetric
1. Add integration tests in tests/ for subcommands using temp jj repos (tempfile crate)
1. Fix .claude repo history: dev0 through dev2 sessions squashed into wrong commit [4],[5]
1. Add `vc-x1 setup` subcommand: completions install, .claude repo init, symlink setup [27]
1. Add dynamic revision completion via `ArgValueCompleter` (jj doesn't complete revsets either) [28],[29]

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

  the current state make sense / pass health checks".
- Distinct commands, distinct concerns. Both worth having.

## Generalize --scope across commands (0.40.0)

Foundation for item #1 on todo ([60]): commands should support
single-repo, dual-repo, and plain-old-repo (POR) workspaces
without hard-coding the `.claude` dual-repo assumption.

**Scope default resolution** (no `--scope` given):

- No `.vc-config.toml` → `--scope=app`.
- `.vc-config.toml` present but `other-repo` missing or empty
  → `--scope=app`.
- `.vc-config.toml` with a non-empty `other-repo` → `--scope=both`.

**Ambiguity rule.** Any ambiguous combination of scope-related
flags on a single invocation (e.g. `-R` with `--scope`) is a
fatal error — callers must pick one way to express repo
selection per call.

**Cycle steps.**

- **0.40.0-0** — this plan. Notes + version bump only.
- **0.40.0-1** — `vc-x1 init --scope=app|other|both`.
  - `app` → single-repo workspace (`.vc-config.toml` with
    `path = "/"` and no `other-repo`; no `.claude` subdir; one
    GitHub repo; no `ochid:` trailer on the initial commit).
  - `both` (default) → current dual-repo behavior.
  - `other` → error; meaningless at init time.
  - Every other subcommand errors loudly if `--scope` is passed.
- Subsequent `-N` steps wire `--scope` into sync, push,
  finalize, and the read-only commands. Specifics decided when
  each lands.

**Helper placement is deliberately open** — `common.rs`,
`init.rs`, or a dedicated `scope.rs` / `scope_helpers.rs` are
all fine for step `-1`; we can refactor later once more call
sites exist. The plan is subject to change as we learn; `-0`
records the current best guess, not a contract.

# References

content.text:
`notes/README.md` doesn't reference init's flags, so no update there.

## Step `-1` plan

**New file `src/scope.rs`** (small, ~20 lines):
- `Scope` enum: `App`, `Other`, `Both` with clap `ValueEnum` derive + brief module doc.

**`src/main.rs`:** add `mod scope;`.

**`src/init.rs`:**
- Add `scope: Option<Scope>` to `InitArgs` (default `None`; init resolves to `Both` to preserve current behavior — workspace doesn't exist yet at init time, so the "workspace-state-driven default" rule doesn't apply here).
- Add constants `VC_CONFIG_APP_ONLY` (only `path = "/"`, no `other-repo`) and `GITIGNORE_APP_ONLY` (no `/.claude` line).
- Refactor `init()` into a small dispatcher + `init_app()` + `init_both()` (current flow renamed into `init_both` verbatim). Keeps the dual-repo path bit-identical to today.
- `init_app()` does the same 11-ish steps but: no session dir, no session `git init`/`jj init`, no second `.vc-config.toml`, no session template, no `ochid:` trailer on the initial commit (since there's no counterpart), single GitHub repo create+push, no symlink setup.
- `--scope=other` at init errors ("meaningless at init — no app repo to anchor to; use `--scope=app` or `--scope=both`").
- `--scope=app` + `--use-template CODE,BOT` errors per the ambiguity rule ("`--scope=app` takes a single template; got `CODE,BOT`").
- Tests: `--scope` arg parsing (all three values + unset), new constants' content, and the `--scope=app` + `,BOT` rejection.

**`notes/chores-06.md`:** append `### 0.40.0-1: vc-x1 init --scope` subsection under the existing umbrella, documenting what shipped.

**`Cargo.toml`:** bump to `0.40.0-1`.

## Other commands: how they "error"

No flag additions elsewhere. Clap natively rejects unknown args — `vc-x1 sync --scope=app` gives `error: unexpected argument '--scope' found`. Per your "all other commands it's an error" direction, that's sufficient for `-1`. Friendlier per-command errors can land when each command wires scope for real.

## Open question

`gh_repo_exists` precheck: in app mode, only check for the single `code_repo` (not `<name>.claude`). That's what I'm planning. Confirm?

If all of this looks right, say "go" and I'll start editing.

message.content: hmm, actually we should do test-fixture first or we should allow `-R <path|url>` and support `file:///test-repo` or `/tmp/xxx` or `~/zzz` or `../def` or  `git@github.com:winksaville/test-repo`, thoughts?

content.text:
Good catch — `init_app` as written can't be dogfooded without hitting real GitHub. Two ways out, and I lean toward the second:

**Option A — test-fixture first.** Build a `test-fixture --scope=app` (or similar) that stages a local bare repo as the "remote" and exercises `init_app` against it. Keeps `gh repo create` coupling in `init`; pushes the test-harness complexity.

**Option B — `-R <url|path>` on `init` (my pick).** Accept any git-remote-ish target as the origin: `/tmp/xxx.git`, `file:///tmp/xxx.git`, `~/zzz.git`, `../def.git`, `git@github.com:foo/bar.git`, `https://…`. When `-R` is given, skip `gh repo create` entirely and use the supplied URL as `origin`. When it's absent, keep today's behavior (`gh repo create` + derived SSH URL).

Why B:
1. Dogfood + local bare-repo testing fall out for free — no special test-fixture wiring.
2. Unblocks non-GitHub users (GitLab, self-hosted, offline) as a side effect.
3. GitHub coupling becomes a branch, not a requirement. Cleaner layering.
4. Orthogonal to `--scope` — works for `app` and `both` alike.

**Semantic note on the flag letter:** `-R`/`--repo` is already used project-wide for "which jj repo to operate on" (chid/desc/list/show/sync/fix-desc/validate-desc). Reusing `-R` on `init` for "where's the git remote" overloads the letter. I'd rename to `--remote <URL>` to keep the `-R` meaning consistent. Normalization: tilde expansion (`~/zzz` → `$HOME/zzz`), relative → absolute, `file://` stripped to a path. SSH URLs and `https://` pass through untouched. Ambiguity rule (`--remote` + auto-GitHub detection) → if `--remote` is set, we never call `gh repo create`, full stop.

**Impact on the in-flight `-1` step:** the `init_app` body I just wrote needs a `match --remote { Some → just set origin + push; None → current gh-create flow }` branch. ~30 extra lines, straightforward. Same structure applies (eventually) to `init_both` for symmetry, though I'd keep that out of `-1`.

**Revised plan for `-1`:**
- `--scope=app|other|both` on `init` (current work).
- `--remote <URL>` on `init` — active for `--scope=app` this step; for `--scope=both` the flag errors "not yet wired for --scope=both" (consistent with your "other commands it's an error" posture).
- Test: unit-level URL/path normalization; plus an end-to-end `init --scope=app --remote /tmp/...` run against a bare-repo fixture we set up inline.

If you're good with B, I'll extend the current working copy (already has `init_app` + `--scope`) with `--remote` support and finish step `-1` without committing yet. If you'd rather A, I'll roll back the `-1` edits unshipped and switch to test-fixture work.

content.text:
You're right that my ordering is backwards — I was layering `--scope` on top of a still-GitHub-coupled `init`, then patching with `--remote` to make the new branch testable. The cleaner dependency order is: generalize the remote-ness first, then add scope on top of a flag set that already works.

**On the two flags:** like them. Clean separation of concerns:

- `--repo-local <path>` — where the on-disk repo goes. Today derived from positional `NAME` + `--dir`. With this flag you can skip that dance: `init --repo-local /tmp/myproj` and name is the basename.
- `--repo-remote <url>` — where `origin` points. Today derived from `owner/NAME` + `gh repo create`. With this flag, skip `gh` entirely and use the given URL directly (local bare repo, SSH, HTTPS, `file://`, `~/` — all normalized to absolute paths or passed through).

Defaults compose nicely: neither flag = today's behavior; either alone = that side explicit, other derived; both = fully qualified, positional `NAME` becomes optional (or an ambiguity error if also given).

**Dual-repo shape (`--scope=both`):** two locals, two remotes. Follow `--use-template`'s `CODE[,BOT]` pattern — `--repo-local CODE[,BOT]`, `--repo-remote CODE[,BOT]`, BOT derived via `.claude` suffix if omitted. Keeps the existing mental model.

**Flag name check:** `--repo-local` / `--repo-remote` reads well and doesn't collide with `-R`/`--repo` used elsewhere for jj-repo selection. The `repo-` prefix earns its keep.

**Ambiguity rules (pre-flight validation):**
- `NAME` + fully-qualified `--repo-local` → error (two ways to derive the basename).
- `--repo-remote` + `gh`-specific flags (`--owner`, `--private`) → error when `--repo-remote` skips GitHub.
- Dual-repo `--repo-remote CODE,BOT` where one side is a local path and the other is an SSH URL → allowed, just a mixed workspace. Don't over-restrict.

## Proposed step ordering

- **0.40.0-1 (revised)** — `--repo-local` + `--repo-remote` on `init`, dual-repo only. Current behavior preserved when both flags are absent. Bare-repo fixture tests land with this step. No `--scope` yet.
- **0.40.0-2** — `--scope=app|other|both` on `init`. Layers on cleanly because step `-1` already split "local path" from "remote URL." `init_app` becomes a small variant of the same flow, not a parallel stack.

This means reverting the `init_app` / `--scope` edits in the current working copy before I start `-1`. The chores-06 `-0` plan subsection would get an amendment (`-1` and `-2` swap responsibilities). Given 0.40.0-0 is already pushed, that amendment lands as a small note-only tweak inside the `-1` commit, or as its own `-0.1` — your call, but I'd fold it into `-1` to avoid ceremony.

Want me to roll back the current `init_app`/scope work and start fresh on `-1` with just the two flags?

content.text:
Rollback clean — `src/scope.rs` gone, `src/main.rs` and `src/init.rs` back to `0.40.0-0` state. Only `Cargo.toml` stays bumped at `0.40.0-1`.

## Revised step `-1` plan

**`src/init.rs` surface changes:**

Two new flags on `InitArgs`:

- `--repo-local <PATH[,PATH]>` — on-disk placement. Single path in future `--scope=app`; `CODE[,BOT]` comma form today (dual-repo). BOT derives as `<CODE>.claude` when omitted (same rule as `--use-template`).
- `--repo-remote <URL[,URL]>` — `origin` URL for each repo. Same comma-form shape. When present, skip `gh repo create` entirely for that side.

**Make positional `NAME` optional.** Today it's required. With the new flags:
- `--repo-local` alone → derive NAME from basename of CODE.
- NAME alone → today's behavior (derive `<cwd>/<NAME>` and `<cwd>/<NAME>/.claude`).
- Both given → ambiguity error.
- Neither given → clap error ("NAME or --repo-local required").

**Normalization helper** `normalize_remote(s) -> String`:
- `~/...` expands via `$HOME`.
- Relative paths (`../x.git`, `./x.git`, `x.git`) → canonical absolute.
- `file:///abs` → pass through (git understands it natively).
- `http(s)://`, `ssh://`, `git@host:path` → pass through.
- Bare absolute paths (`/tmp/x.git`) → pass through.

**Preflight changes:**
- `gh auth status` only runs when at least one side needs `gh repo create` (i.e., `--repo-remote` absent for that side).
- `gh_whoami` called only when needed (same gate).
- `gh_repo_exists` precheck skipped per side when `--repo-remote` supplies that side.

**Ambiguity rules:**
- `NAME` + `--repo-local` → error.
- `--repo-remote` covers all sides + `--owner` → error (no GitHub derivation happening).
- `--repo-remote` covers all sides + `--private` → error (visibility is a `gh repo create` concept).
- `--repo-local A,B` with one path missing (`,B` or `A,`) → error.

**Refactor shape inside `init()` body:**
- Pre-resolve `(project_dir, session_dir)` from whichever source applies.
- Pre-resolve `(code_remote_url, session_remote_url)` — `Some(url)` if supplied, `None` means "we'll create via `gh`."
- Each `gh repo create` call + `gh_repo_exists` precheck becomes gated on the `None` case.
- `git remote add origin <url>` uses the supplied-or-derived URL uniformly.
- `debug!(…)` documentary lines above each `run()` call in the touched paths (new convention from your last instruction).

**Tests:**
- Clap parsing: `--repo-local=A`, `--repo-local=A,B`, `--repo-remote=URL`, `--repo-remote=URL1,URL2`, missing halves error.
- `normalize_remote` unit tests — each case above.
- Ambiguity rejections for NAME-plus-local, remote-plus-owner, remote-plus-private.
- **End-to-end:** create two bare `git init --bare` repos in tempdir, invoke `init` with `--repo-remote` pointing at them, assert: project tree exists, both `.vc-config.toml`s are correct, both commits are present in the bare repos, `origin` remotes match, jj bookmark tracking is set up. No `gh` invocation should happen — confirmed by running with `PATH` scrubbed of `gh` (or by asserting a mock not-called).

**`notes/chores-06.md`:**
- Amend the `## Generalize --scope across commands (0.40.0)` section's step list: `-1` becomes "init remote decoupling via `--repo-local` + `--repo-remote`"; `-2` becomes the scope work; later steps renumber.
- Append `### 0.40.0-1: init remote decoupling` subsection documenting what shipped.

**Out of scope for `-1`:** no `--scope` flag yet; no `Scope` enum; no `init_app`. All that lands in `-2`.

## Open questions

1. `NAME` optional: OK to make the positional optional this step? It's a small CLI-surface change but enables the clean "fully qualified via flags" case.
2. Tilde expansion: use `$HOME` or attempt a richer expansion (e.g. `~user/…`)? I'd stick to `$HOME` only — matches shell `~` with no username.
3. Should `--repo-local` + `--repo-remote` both taking `CODE[,BOT]` form be documented as "must match shape" (both give 1 value, or both give 2)? I think yes — mismatch is fatal per ambiguity rule.

If this looks right, say go and I'll implement.

The shapes are asymmetric because on-disk structure is nested (one root path suffices) while remote URLs are independent. Fixing the docstring and parse to match.

file.content:
## Generalize --scope across commands (0.40.0)

Foundation for item #1 on todo ([60]): commands should support
single-repo, dual-repo, and plain-old-repo (POR) workspaces
without hard-coding the `.claude` dual-repo assumption.

**Scope default resolution** (no `--scope` given):

- No `.vc-config.toml` → `--scope=app`.
- `.vc-config.toml` present but `other-repo` missing or empty
  → `--scope=app`.
- `.vc-config.toml` with a non-empty `other-repo` → `--scope=both`.

**Ambiguity rule.** Any ambiguous combination of scope-related
flags on a single invocation (e.g. `-R` with `--scope`) is a
fatal error — callers must pick one way to express repo
selection per call.

**Cycle steps.**

- **0.40.0-0** — this plan. Notes + version bump only.
- **0.40.0-1** — `vc-x1 init --scope=app|other|both`.
  - `app` → single-repo workspace (`.vc-config.toml` with
    `path = "/"` and no `other-repo`; no `.claude` subdir; one
    GitHub repo; no `ochid:` trailer on the initial commit).
  - `both` (default) → current dual-repo behavior.
  - `other` → error; meaningless at init time.
  - Every other subcommand errors loudly if `--scope` is passed.
- Subsequent `-N` steps wire `--scope` into sync, push,
  finalize, and the read-only commands. Specifics decided when
  each lands.

**Helper placement is deliberately open** — `common.rs`,
`init.rs`, or a dedicated `scope.rs` / `scope_helpers.rs` are
all fine for step `-1`; we can refactor later once more call
sites exist. The plan is subject to change as we learn; `-0`
records the current best guess, not a contract.

# References

[57]: /notes/chores-05.md#capture-squash-mode--scope-design-for-push-0374
[60]: #generalize---scope-to-all-commands-design
[61]: #push-hardening-state--stage-sanity-design
[63]: #non-tracking-remote-bookmark-detection-design
[64]: #notes-restructure-chores-06--trim-long-todo-entries-0377
[65]: #scope-design-refinements-0378
[66]: #bookmark-tracking-verification-0380
[67]: #push-hardening-state--stage-sanity-0390
[68]: #source-code-design-ref-convention-design
[69]: #vc-x1-validate-repo-command-design

input.new_string:
**Cycle steps.**

- **0.40.0-0** — this plan. Notes + version bump only.
- **0.40.0-1** — `vc-x1 init --repo-local` + `--repo-remote`.
  Decouple init from `gh repo create` so it can target any git
  remote (local bare repo, `file:///…`, SSH, HTTPS) — both as a
  step toward POR / single-repo support and so dogfooding +
  testing can run without hitting real GitHub. Dual-repo only;
  no `--scope` yet. **Ordering rationale (added 2026-04-24):**
  doing `--scope=app` before this step would build on a still
  GitHub-coupled init and immediately need rework once
  `--repo-remote` lands; flipping the order means `-2` lands
  cleanly on top of an already remote-agnostic flow.
- **0.40.0-2** — `vc-x1 init --scope=app|other|both`.
  - `app` → single-repo workspace (`.vc-config.toml` with
    `path = "/"` and no `other-repo`; no `.claude` subdir; one
    GitHub repo (or one `--repo-remote`); no `ochid:` trailer
    on the initial commit).
  - `both` (default) → current dual-repo behavior.
  - `other` → error; meaningless at init time.
  - Every other subcommand errors loudly if `--scope` is passed.
- Subsequent `-N` steps wire `--scope` into sync, push,
  finalize, and the read-only commands. Specifics decided when
  each lands.

**Helper placement is deliberately open** — `common.rs`,
`init.rs`, or a dedicated `scope.rs` / `scope_helpers.rs` are
all fine; we can refactor later once more call sites exist. The
plan is subject to change as we learn; `-0` records the current
best guess, not a contract.

### 0.40.0-1: init remote decoupling

Two new flags on `vc-x1 init` to break the hard coupling to
`gh repo create`:

- `--repo-local <PATH>` — single path; replaces the on-disk
  project root. Session repo always lands at `<PATH>/.claude`
  (the nested layout is hardcoded in `.vc-config.toml`'s
  `other-repo = ".claude"` and `path = "/.claude"` fields).
  Mutually exclusive with the positional `NAME` and `--dir`.
  The positional `NAME` is now optional at the clap layer; the
  dispatcher errors at runtime if neither is supplied.
- `--repo-remote <CODE,BOT>` — both URLs explicit (no
  derivation; URL conventions vary too much to magic-derive).
  Each value may be a bare path, `file:///…`, an HTTPS URL, or
  an SSH URL (`git@host:path`). `~/…` and `$VAR` / `${VAR}`
  are expanded; relative paths canonicalize against cwd; URLs
  pass through. Mutually exclusive with `--owner` and
  `--private` (both are `gh repo create`-only concepts). When
  `--repo-remote` is present, init no longer requires the `gh`
  CLI to be installed or authenticated.

**Asymmetric value shapes are intentional.** `--repo-local`
takes one path because the on-disk layout is nested.
`--repo-remote` takes two because remote URLs are independent.
There is no shape-matching rule between the two flags.

**Helper module is `init.rs`.** No `scope.rs` yet — that lands
in `-2`. Helpers added: `expand_vars`, `is_remote_url`,
`normalize_local_path`, `normalize_remote`, `split_code_bot`,
plus two private resolvers `resolve_project_dir` and
`resolve_remotes`.

**Documentary `debug!` lines** — every `run()` invocation in
the touched paths now has a `debug!(…)` immediately above it
that captures the *intent* of the call (the *action* is logged
by `common::run` itself). Exists in source as documentation
and in the `-v` log as runtime breadcrumbs. New convention
(2026-04-24); applied to the `init()` body in this step,
retrofit-able elsewhere later.

- `Cargo.toml`: bump to `0.40.0-1`.
- `src/init.rs`: new flags, helpers, dispatcher refactor, and
  documentary `debug!` retrofit.
- `notes/todo.md`: ref bookkeeping unchanged (item still in
  progress under `[60],[70]`).
- `notes/chores-06.md`: amend `-0` plan ordering rationale +
  this subsection.

  pass through. Mutually exclusive with `--owner` and
  `--private` (both are `gh repo create`-only concepts). When
  `--repo-remote` is present, init no longer requires the `gh`
  CLI to be installed or authenticated.

**Asymmetric value shapes are intentional.** `--repo-local`
takes one path because the on-disk layout is nested.
`--repo-remote` takes two because remote URLs are independent.
There is no shape-matching rule between the two flags.

**Helper module is `init.rs`.** No `scope.rs` yet — that lands
in `-2`. Helpers added: `expand_vars`, `is_remote_url`,
`normalize_local_path`, `normalize_remote`, `split_code_bot`,
plus two private resolvers `resolve_project_dir` and
`resolve_remotes`.

**Documentary `debug!` lines** — every `run()` invocation in
the touched paths now has a `debug!(…)` immediately above it
that captures the *intent* of the call (the *action* is logged
by `common::run` itself). Exists in source as documentation
and in the `-v` log as runtime breadcrumbs. New convention
(2026-04-24); applied to the `init()` body in this step,
retrofit-able elsewhere later.

- `notes/todo.md`: ref bookkeeping unchanged (item still in
