content.text:
End-to-end works. All 11 steps ran, both bare repos received their initial commit, origin URLs point at the bare repos, and no `gh` calls happened (the absence of "Creating GitHub repo..." messages confirms it).

## Summary of step `-1` changes

**`src/init.rs`** — substantial:
- New flags `--repo-local <PATH>` and `--repo-remote <CODE,BOT>` on `InitArgs`. Positional `NAME` is now `Option<String>` (clap-optional).
- New helpers: `expand_vars`, `is_remote_url`, `normalize_local_path`, `normalize_remote`, `split_code_bot`, plus dispatch helpers `resolve_project_dir` and `resolve_remotes`.
- `init()` body refactored: ambiguity rules enforced up front (NAME+repo-local, dir+repo-local, repo-remote+owner, repo-remote+private, repo-local-with-comma); `gh` calls and the `gh_whoami`/`gh_repo_exists`/`gh repo create`/visibility paths gated behind `using_gh = explicit_remotes.is_none()`; `git remote add origin` uses the resolved URL uniformly. Dry-run output reports either "Step 8/9: gh repo create" or "Step 8/9: skip gh repo create; push to ...".
- Documentary `debug!(…)` lines added above each `run()` call in `init()`.
- 16 new tests covering arg parsing, helper behavior, and ambiguity rejections. Existing tests updated for `Option<String>` name.

**`Cargo.toml`** — `0.40.0-0` → `0.40.0-1`.

**`notes/chores-06.md`** — amended `-0` plan with the ordering rationale, swapped `-1`/`-2` step responsibilities, appended `### 0.40.0-1: init remote decoupling` describing the shipped surface and conventions.

**Verified:**
- `cargo fmt`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean.
- `cargo test` — 232 passed (216 → 232).
- `cargo install --path . --locked` succeeded.
- Dry-run smoke test: `vc-x1 init smoke-proj --dir … --repo-remote a.git,b.git --dry-run` shows `Step 8/9: skip gh repo create`, no `gh auth status` invoked.
- End-to-end smoke test: full real run against two local bare repos. Both repos received the initial commit; origin URLs correctly set; no `gh` invocations; nested `.claude` layout intact.

**Out of scope, deferred:**
- Automated end-to-end test (would need `$HOME` override to avoid polluting `~/.claude/projects/` with the Step 11 symlink).
- `--scope` flag (lands in `-2`).

Work complete. Please review. On approval I'll prep the commit title and body for a second approval before pushing.

content: Goal: generalize vc-x1 to operate on any repo, not just dual-repo. Current task: step 0.40.0-1 adds --repo-local and --repo-remote to init, code complete and tested. Next: get your approval to commit and push. (disable recaps in /config)

message.content: Miss communication, when I said --repo-local I meant local like in test-fixture so nothing created externally, that wasn't obvious. So `vc-x1 init tf1 --repo-local /tmp/xyz` would create /tmp/xyz/tf1, /tmp/xyz/remote-claude.git and /tmp/xyz/remote-code.git. And then `vc-x1 init tf1 --repo-remote git@github.com:winksaville/tf1` is the same as the default `vc-x1 init tf1`. That was my thought then we'd remove `test-fixture`?

content.text:
Got it — I had `--repo-local` completely wrong. Restating the corrected semantics to make sure we're aligned:

## Corrected design

**`--repo-local <PARENT>`** — fully self-contained workspace, no GitHub, no external `gh` calls. Creates:
- `<PARENT>/<NAME>/` — project (code side)
- `<PARENT>/<NAME>/.claude/` — session (nested as today)
- `<PARENT>/remote-code.git` — bare repo, used as code's `origin`
- `<PARENT>/remote-claude.git` — bare repo, used as session's `origin`

**`--repo-remote <URL>`** — single URL stem; both sides derived. `<URL>.git` for code, `<URL>.claude.git` for session. Skips `gh repo create` entirely; the URL must already point at writable repos. So `vc-x1 init tf1 --repo-remote git@github.com:winksaville/tf1` is exactly today's `vc-x1 init tf1` (when owner=`winksaville`) — just lets you spell the URL when the default derivation doesn't fit.

**Default (neither flag)** — today's behavior: `gh whoami` + `gh repo create` + derived SSH URL.

**Mutually exclusive.** `--repo-local` and `--repo-remote` together → fatal. NAME stays required in all three modes.

## On retiring `test-fixture`

Yes — once `--repo-local` lands, `test-fixture` is largely redundant. But I'd not bundle the removal into `-1`:

- `test-fixture` has callers throughout `src/sync.rs` and `src/push.rs` integration tests. Migrating those to `init --repo-local` is its own change with its own risk surface.
- There may be subtle differences: `test-fixture` creates a fixture with specific bookmark state and bare-remote layout that the integration tests assume. `init --repo-local` makes one initial commit. We'll need to verify the structures match before swapping.
- Step `-1` should ship one decoupling change cleanly — adding the flags. Migrating callers is a follow-up.

**Suggested cycle re-shape:**
- **0.40.0-1** (revised once more): `--repo-local PARENT` + `--repo-remote URL` on `init`, with the corrected semantics above. Mutually exclusive. NAME required.
- **0.40.0-2**: `--scope=app|other|both` (was the original `-1`).
- **0.40.0-3**: migrate `sync` and `push` integration tests from `test_fixture` to `init --repo-local`. Verify equivalence per test; adjust where shapes differ.
- **0.40.0-4**: delete the `test_fixture` module (and the `test-fixture` / `test-fixture-rm` subcommands) once no callers remain.
- Subsequent steps: wire `--scope` into sync, push, finalize, etc. (as before).

Test-fixture retirement is two steps because (a) migrating tests is the risky bit and (b) keeping module + commands alive across one step lets us run both old and new harnesses side-by-side if anything looks off during the migration.

## Path forward for the current working copy

The flags and dispatch logic I just wrote have the wrong shapes — value parsing, the `split_code_bot` helper, the resolution functions, and most of the new tests are all built around `CODE,BOT` two-value flags. Cheaper to roll back to `0.40.0-0` state and redo than to mutate in place. The helpers `expand_vars`, `is_remote_url`, and `normalize_remote` survive (still useful); `normalize_local_path` and `split_code_bot` come out.

Want me to roll back and redo `-1` with the corrected design? Or any further tweaks to the design first?

message.content: Do you agree for me, winksaville, `vc-x1 init --repo-remote git@github.com:winksaville/tf1` == `vc-x1 init tf1 --owner winksaville` == `vc-x1 init tf1`?

content.text:
Almost — but there's a subtle wedge depending on how `--repo-remote` handles `gh repo create`.

**End-state equivalence (after init):** Yes — same project tree on disk, same remote URLs, same pushed commits.

**Step semantics:** Differ depending on a design call:

- `vc-x1 init tf1` → derives `git@github.com:winksaville/tf1.git`; calls `gh repo create`; pushes.
- `vc-x1 init tf1 --owner winksaville` → same as above (just skips `gh whoami`).
- `vc-x1 init --repo-remote git@github.com:winksaville/tf1` → uses the URL directly; **whether it calls `gh repo create` is a design call**.

Two ways to resolve the wedge:

**(a) Make them strictly equivalent.** Treat default as syntactic sugar over `--repo-remote <derived-URL>`. `--repo-remote` then has to detect GitHub URLs by host and call `gh repo create` for them (skipping for non-GitHub URLs and for paths). NAME is derived from the URL's last path segment (strip trailing `.git`) when not given positionally. This is what makes #1 == #2 == #3 hold literally.

**(b) Keep `--repo-remote` as a "bring your own remote" escape hatch.** Always skip `gh repo create` when `--repo-remote` is set; the URL must already point at a writable repo. Then #1 != #2/#3 — same end-state IFF the user pre-created the remote, but the steps `init` runs differ.

I lean toward **(a)**, because:
1. It collapses default + `--repo-remote` + `--owner` into one code path with parameters, removing the "two modes" mental model.
2. URL host detection is small (`s.starts_with("git@github.com:") || s.contains("://github.com/")`).
3. Non-GitHub URL handling falls out naturally — no magic for those, user pre-creates.
4. NAME derivation from URL ("derive when omitted, error if both given and they disagree") is a clean ambiguity rule.

The cost is one extra branch (URL-is-github) in the `should-I-call-gh-repo-create` check. Worth it for the unified semantics.

Under (a), the answer to your question is **yes, all three are equivalent** for winksaville.

Under (b), the answer is **same final remote URLs, but `gh repo create` runs in #2/#3 only**.

Which way do you want it?

content: Adding `--repo-local` and `--repo-remote` to `vc-x1 init` (step 0.40.0-1, foundation for `--scope` work) so init can target local bare repos or arbitrary URLs without `gh`. Next: pick design (a) unified or (b) escape-hatch for `--repo-remote`'s `gh repo create` behavior. (disable recaps in /config)

message.content: I'm think (a) too, but also I'd like this to work on gitlab or any other git based system local or remote. In the short term you might have to "pre-create" the repo itself but that would be ok too, thoughts?

content.text:
Yes — option (a) with a tiered provisioner pattern. Making the design explicitly extensible:

## Unified model

The URL (whether given or derived) runs through a **provisioner dispatch**:

```
provisioner(url, repo_local) =
    if repo_local          → LocalBareInit    (git init --bare <parent>/remote-*.git)
    if is_github_url(url)  → GhCreate         (gh repo create)
    else                   → ExternalPreExisting  (skip — user pre-created)
```

**Default case** becomes syntactic sugar: `init tf1` ≡ `init tf1 --repo-remote git@github.com:<gh-whoami>/tf1`. Same code path, same dispatch.

**Host detection** for step `-1` is literal: GitHub (SSH + HTTPS). Future hosts (gitlab, gitea, self-hosted) slot in as additional arms — no restructuring needed. Short-term, non-GitHub users pre-create; that's fine. A `glab repo create` arm can land whenever someone needs it.

**Preflight per provisioner:**
- `GhCreate` — `gh auth status`, then `gh_repo_exists` (error if repo already exists, same as today).
- `LocalBareInit` — parent dir must exist; the bare-repo paths must NOT exist (refuse to clobber).
- `ExternalPreExisting` — for path-shaped URLs, verify the path exists (error with "use `--repo-local` for fixture creation, or pre-create with `git init --bare`"). For scheme-qualified URLs, no preflight — git push will surface failure.

## Answer to your equivalence question

Under this model, **yes** — #1 == #2 == #3 for winksaville. Same resolved URLs, same dispatch (both derive a GitHub URL, both hit `GhCreate`), same end state.

## Step `-1` implementation shape

**`--repo-local <PARENT>`** — fixture mode. Creates `<PARENT>/<NAME>/`, `<PARENT>/<NAME>/.claude/`, `<PARENT>/remote-code.git` (bare), `<PARENT>/remote-claude.git` (bare). Uses those bare repos as origins. No gh, no network.

**`--repo-remote <URL>`** — single URL stem. Session URL derived per rule: if URL ends in `.git`, insert `.claude` before the suffix; otherwise append `.claude`. NAME derived from URL's last path segment when positional omitted; if both given and they disagree, fatal.

**Mutually exclusive.** `--repo-local` + `--repo-remote` → error.

**NAME derivation rule:**
- URL last segment (after last `/` or `:`), stripped of trailing `.git`.
- `git@github.com:winksaville/tf1` → `tf1`
- `git@github.com:winksaville/tf1.git` → `tf1`
- `https://github.com/winksaville/tf1.git` → `tf1`
- `/tmp/foo.git` → `foo`

**Helpers (keep from last round):** `expand_vars`, `is_remote_url`, `normalize_remote`.
**Helpers (new):** `is_github_url`, `derive_session_url`, `derive_name_from_url`, `Provisioner` enum + dispatch.
**Helpers (drop from last round):** `normalize_local_path` (replaced by fixture-parent handling), `split_code_bot` (no pair form anymore).

1	# Chores-06.md
2	
3	General chores notes — design captures (forward-looking) and
4	post-implementation chore entries. Same shape as chores-01..05.md;
5	06 starts here only because 05 has gotten long.
6	
7	Subsection headers use the trailing-version format from CLAUDE.md
8	when they correspond to a release: `## Description (X.Y.Z)`.
9	Pre-implementation design captures may use a plain title; once
10	implemented, the title can become a release-versioned chore.
11	
12	## Notes restructure: chores-06 + trim long todo entries (0.37.7)
13	
14	Notes-only release. Three doc-hygiene moves bundled:
15	
16	- Start `notes/chores-06.md` (this file). chores-05.md grew to
17	  ~1000 lines; new chore entries land here going forward. No
18	  reservation for design-only — chores-06 is general-purpose.
19	- Trim long todo entries to 2-3 line summaries; move detail to
20	  the relevant chores-05 design subsection or a new chores-06
21	  subsection. One item per top-level list entry, no `##`
22	  subsections, loose topical ordering only — and order
23	  highest-priority-first.
24	- Adopt lazy numbering for Todo items: every entry uses `1. `;
25	  the markdown renderer auto-numbers. Lets the user reference
26	  items by displayed number without manual renumbering on
27	  reorder/insert. Done section stays on `-` bullets — items
28	  aren't referenced by number once completed.
29	
30	Design subsections added below for items that previously lived
31	inline in `notes/todo.md`:
32	
33	- "Generalize --scope to all commands (design)"
34	- "Push hardening: state + stage sanity (design)"
35	- "bm-track silent-when-clean (design)"
36	- "Non-tracking-remote bookmark detection (design)"
37	
38	Also: removed the redundant "Allow `vc-x1 push` to work on code
39	or bot repo together or independantly..." entry from todo —
40	fully subsumed by the `--scope` + `--squash` work.
41	
42	- `notes/chores-06.md` — new file with intro + this chore entry +
43	  four design subsections.
44	- `notes/todo.md` — Todo section rewritten in priority order;
45	  long entries trimmed to short summaries; redundant entry
46	  removed; switched to `1. ` lazy numbering; Done entry +
47	  new `[60]`–`[64]` references added.
48	- `notes/README.md` — `## Todo format` updated with the
49	  lazy-numbering convention note.
50	
51	## Generalize --scope to all commands (design)
52	
53	Surfaced 2026-04-23 (from the user, top of todo): vc-x1 commands
54	should support both single and dual repo modes. The technique
55	sketched for `vc-x1 push` (`--scope=app|claude|both`) is the
56	right shape, but the scope concept should be a project-wide
57	convention rather than push-only.
58	
59	**Naming:** `--scope=app|other|both` — "other" rather than
60	"claude" because the second repo isn't always `.claude`; could
61	be a different bot or a different sibling project structure.
62	
63	**Why scope-first** (before adding more commands or push
64	features): each new command that operates across both repos
65	would otherwise need its own ad-hoc handling. Locking in the
66	convention now means new commands inherit it for free, and
67	existing commands (sync, finalize, push) get retrofitted under
68	the same vocabulary.
69	
70	**Rough scope of the change:**
71	
72	- Shared CLI flag definition + parsing helper (likely in
73	  `common.rs`).
74	- `sync`: already accepts `-R` for single-repo. Migrate to
75	  `--scope`; alias or deprecate `-R` (TBD below).
76	- `push`: `--scope` per the existing design, with
77	  warn-on-WC-mismatch.
78	- `finalize`: already takes `--repo`; rationalize naming.
79	- New commands (`status` etc.) start with `--scope` from day 1.
80	
81	**Open:** do we deprecate `-R` immediately, alias it, or keep
82	both indefinitely? Probably: alias for one minor cycle,
83	deprecate, remove. Decide before implementation.
84	
85	## Push hardening: state + stage sanity (design)
86	
87	Two related correctness fixes for `vc-x1 push` resume behavior.
88	Both surfaced 2026-04-22 from a 0.37.1 dogfood incident and are
89	described together because they tackle the same failure mode
90	(silent false success after stale state).
91	
92	**The incident:** 0.37.1 push errored at finalize-claude;
93	out-of-band recovery (manual finalize + force-push of a squash)
94	moved the world forward; 0.37.2 push then resumed at the parked
95	finalize-claude, no-op'd, and falsely declared "completed all
96	stages" while working copies still held uncommitted changes.
97	
98	**State-sanity preflight on resume.** Before any stage runs,
99	verify saved state matches reality:
100	
101	- `state.app_chid` still exists (not abandoned/rewritten).
102	- `main` bookmark at `state.app_chid`'s commit.
103	- `state.claude_chid` consistent with `.claude` working copy.
104	
105	On mismatch, refuse with a loud "state is stale — run
106	`vc-x1 push --restart`" error.
107	
108	**Stage-prereq verification + honest completion.** Each stage
109	declares what it expects (working-copy dirty for commit-app;
110	bookmark at specific commit for bookmark-both; etc.); the
111	dispatcher checks before running. "Completed all stages" should
112	only print when stages genuinely ran or were
113	verified-already-done, not when they were skipped without
114	verification.
115	
116	**Implementation order:** state-sanity first (broader guard),
117	then per-stage prereqs (more invasive). Both should land before
118	the `--squash` work to avoid building on a known-fragile resume
119	path.
120	
121	## bm-track silent-when-clean (design)
122	
123	Today bm-track prints two lines per command (entry + exit)
124	unconditionally. In steady state both lines say
125	`tracked,tracked` — pure noise. Defer-flagged in 0.37.4 until
126	signal-confidence was established through more dogfood; several
127	0.37.x cycles have now used the probe with no false positives
128	or missed regressions.
129	
130	**Proposed behavior:**
131	
132	- Always probe on entry and exit (no change).
133	- Print on entry only when state isn't fully tracked.
134	- Print on exit when state isn't fully tracked OR when the exit
135	  state differs from entry.
136	- When printing on exit, include the entry state in the output
137	  so the transition is explicit:
138	  `bm-track vc-x1 <cmd>: enter=<state> → exit=<state>`.
139	
140	The entry-state-in-exit-output requirement is important —
141	"entry was ok (not printed), exit failed" is inference;
142	"entry=tracked, exit=NOT_TRACKED" in one line is evidence.
143	
144	**Implementation note:** record entry state in a local variable
145	at the enter call site and pass into the exit call.
146	
147	## Non-tracking-remote bookmark detection (design)
148	
149	Diagnosed 2026-04-22 dogfood: jj's tracking state is
150	**per-workspace** (local `.jj` store), not shared via git refs.
151	Sync across machines transfers refs but not the tracking flag —
152	so a fresh workspace fetched-into never auto-tracks. The failure
153	surfaces only when `jj git push` is attempted, which is too late
154	(push-app already succeeded in our case).
155	
156	**Policy (decided):** error loudly, with the exact
157	`jj bookmark track <b> --remote=<r> -R <repo>` remediation
158	command. No self-heal — keeps the fix explicit and visible.
159	
160	**Scope (every command that creates or mutates repo state):**
161

169	  Verify and fix.
170	- `vc-x1 test-fixture`: Step 7's `jj git push --bookmark main`

172	  Works correctly — confirmed via `jj bookmark list --tracked`

174	  sanity check would naturally cover it.
175	- `vc-x1 sync` preflight: detect + error.
176	- `vc-x1 push` preflight: detect + error (before any mutation).
177	- `vc-x1 finalize`: detect + error before the squash, so a

## Generalize --scope across commands (0.40.0)

Foundation for item #1 on todo ([60]): commands should support
single-repo, dual-repo, and plain-old-repo (POR) workspaces
without hard-coding the `.claude` dual-repo assumption.

**Scope default resolution** (no `--scope` given):

- No `.vc-config.toml` → `--scope=app`.
- `.vc-config.toml` present but `other-repo` missing or empty
  → `--scope=app`.
- `.vc-config.toml` with a non-empty `other-repo` → `--scope=both`.

**Ambiguity rule.** Any ambiguous combination of scope-related
flags on a single invocation (e.g. `-R` with `--scope`) is a
fatal error — callers must pick one way to express repo
selection per call.

**Cycle steps.**

- **0.40.0-0** — this plan. Notes + version bump only.
- **0.40.0-1** — `vc-x1 init --scope=app|other|both`.
  - `app` → single-repo workspace (`.vc-config.toml` with
    `path = "/"` and no `other-repo`; no `.claude` subdir; one
    GitHub repo; no `ochid:` trailer on the initial commit).
  - `both` (default) → current dual-repo behavior.
  - `other` → error; meaningless at init time.
  - Every other subcommand errors loudly if `--scope` is passed.
- Subsequent `-N` steps wire `--scope` into sync, push,
  finalize, and the read-only commands. Specifics decided when
  each lands.

**Helper placement is deliberately open** — `common.rs`,
`init.rs`, or a dedicated `scope.rs` / `scope_helpers.rs` are
all fine for step `-1`; we can refactor later once more call
sites exist. The plan is subject to change as we learn; `-0`
records the current best guess, not a contract.

# References

[57]: /notes/chores-05.md#capture-squash-mode--scope-design-for-push-0374
[60]: #generalize---scope-to-all-commands-design
[61]: #push-hardening-state--stage-sanity-design
[63]: #non-tracking-remote-bookmark-detection-design
[64]: #notes-restructure-chores-06--trim-long-todo-entries-0377
[65]: #scope-design-refinements-0378
[66]: #bookmark-tracking-verification-0380
[67]: #push-hardening-state--stage-sanity-0390
[68]: #source-code-design-ref-convention-design
[69]: #vc-x1-validate-repo-command-design

## Generalize --scope across commands (0.40.0)

Foundation for item #1 on todo ([60]). Commands should support
single-repo, dual-repo, and plain-old-repo (POR) workspaces
without hard-coding the `.claude` dual-repo assumption.

**Scope default resolution** (no `--scope` given):

- No `.vc-config.toml` → `--scope=app`.
- `.vc-config.toml` with `other-repo` missing/empty → `--scope=app`.
- `.vc-config.toml` with a non-empty `other-repo` → `--scope=both`.

**Ambiguity rule.** Any ambiguous combination of scope-related
flags on one invocation is a fatal error — callers pick exactly
one way to express repo selection per call.

**Cycle steps.**

- **0.40.0-0** — this plan. Notes + version bump only.
- **0.40.0-1** — `vc-x1 init --repo-local` + `--repo-remote`.
  - Decouple init from `gh repo create`; unblock testable
    fixture-mode workflows and non-GitHub remotes.
  - **Ordering rationale (2026-04-24):** doing `--scope=app`
    first would build on a still-GitHub-coupled init and
    immediately need rework; flipping the order means `-2`
    lands cleanly on a remote-agnostic flow.
- **0.40.0-2** — `vc-x1 init --scope=app|other|both`.
  - `app` → single-repo workspace (no `.claude` subdir; one
    repo; no `ochid:` trailer on the initial commit).
  - `both` (default) → current dual-repo behavior.
  - `other` → fatal; meaningless at init time.
  - Other subcommands error if `--scope` is passed.
- Subsequent `-N` steps wire `--scope` into sync, push,
  finalize, read-only commands. Specifics decided per step.

**Helper placement** is deliberately open.

- `common.rs`, `init.rs`, or `scope.rs` / `scope_helpers.rs`
  are all fine; refactor later once more call sites exist.
- Plan is subject to change; `-0` records best guess, not a
  contract.

### 0.40.0-1: init remote decoupling

Two new flags on `vc-x1 init` plus a unified
`Provisioner` dispatch that subsumes the existing default mode.

**Flags.**

- `--repo-local <PARENT>` — fixture mode. Creates
  `<PARENT>/<NAME>/`, `<PARENT>/<NAME>/.claude/`,
  `<PARENT>/remote-code.git`, `<PARENT>/remote-claude.git`.
  No `gh`, no network. Supports `~/…`, `$VAR`, `${VAR}`.
- `--repo-remote <URL>` — single URL stem. Session URL
  derived (insert `.claude` before trailing `.git`, else
  append `.claude`). Supports paths, `file:///…`, HTTPS,
  SSH (scp-like and `ssh://`), and `~/…`/`$VAR`.

**Equivalence (winksaville case):** `vc-x1 init tf1`,
`vc-x1 init tf1 --owner winksaville`, and
`vc-x1 init --repo-remote git@github.com:winksaville/tf1`
all resolve to the same plan and execute identically.

**Provisioner dispatch.** All three modes feed an `InitPlan`
with `provisioner ∈ {LocalBareInit, GhCreate,
ExternalPreExisting}`.

- `LocalBareInit` — `--repo-local`. `git init --bare` on the
  two bare paths.
- `GhCreate` — default mode, or `--repo-remote` with a
  GitHub URL (host detection on the URL string). `gh repo
  create` for both sides.
- `ExternalPreExisting` — `--repo-remote` with a non-GitHub
  URL/path. No create step; caller pre-creates.

**Ambiguity rejections (fatal).**

- `--repo-local` ⊕ `--repo-remote`.
- `--repo-local` ⊕ `--dir` / `--owner` / `--private`.
- `--repo-remote` ⊕ `--owner` / `--private`.
- `NAME` and `--repo-remote` URL with disagreeing names.
- `--repo-local` without `NAME`.

**NAME derivation.** `--repo-remote` URL's last path segment
(trailing `.git` stripped) is used when positional `NAME` is
absent. NAME stays optional at the clap layer; runtime
errors cover the missing-required cases.

**Documentary `debug!` lines** — every `run()` call in `init`
has a `debug!(…)` immediately above it documenting intent
(while `common::run` already logs the action). New convention
(2026-04-24); applied to `init` here, retrofit later if useful.

**Future steps unblocked.**

  to `init --repo-local`.
- `-4` retires `test_fixture` once no callers remain.

- `src/init.rs`: new flags, `Provisioner` enum, `InitPlan`,
  `plan_init` dispatcher, `run_remote_step`, helpers

## Generalize --scope across commands (0.40.0)

Foundation for item #1 on todo ([60]): commands should support
single-repo, dual-repo, and plain-old-repo (POR) workspaces
without hard-coding the `.claude` dual-repo assumption.

**Scope default resolution** (no `--scope` given):

- No `.vc-config.toml` → `--scope=app`.
- `.vc-config.toml` present but `other-repo` missing or empty
  → `--scope=app`.
- `.vc-config.toml` with a non-empty `other-repo` → `--scope=both`.

**Ambiguity rule.** Any ambiguous combination of scope-related
flags on a single invocation (e.g. `-R` with `--scope`) is a
fatal error — callers must pick one way to express repo
selection per call.

**Cycle steps.**

- **0.40.0-0** — this plan. Notes + version bump only.
- **0.40.0-1** — `vc-x1 init --scope=app|other|both`.
  - `app` → single-repo workspace (`.vc-config.toml` with
    `path = "/"` and no `other-repo`; no `.claude` subdir; one
    GitHub repo; no `ochid:` trailer on the initial commit).
  - `both` (default) → current dual-repo behavior.
  - `other` → error; meaningless at init time.
  - Every other subcommand errors loudly if `--scope` is passed.
- Subsequent `-N` steps wire `--scope` into sync, push,
  finalize, and the read-only commands. Specifics decided when
  each lands.

**Helper placement is deliberately open** — `common.rs`,
`init.rs`, or a dedicated `scope.rs` / `scope_helpers.rs` are
all fine for step `-1`; we can refactor later once more call
sites exist. The plan is subject to change as we learn; `-0`
records the current best guess, not a contract.

## Generalize --scope across commands (0.40.0)

Foundation for item #1 on todo ([60]). Commands should support
single-repo, dual-repo, and plain-old-repo (POR) workspaces
without hard-coding the `.claude` dual-repo assumption.

**Cycle steps.**

- **0.40.0-0** — this plan. Notes + version bump only.
- **0.40.0-1** — `vc-x1 init --repo-local` + `--repo-remote`.

    fixture-mode workflows and non-GitHub remotes.
  - **Ordering rationale (2026-04-24):** doing `--scope=app`
    first would build on a still-GitHub-coupled init and
    immediately need rework; flipping the order means `-2`
    lands cleanly on a remote-agnostic flow.

  - `other` → fatal; meaningless at init time.
  - Other subcommands error if `--scope` is passed.
- Subsequent `-N` steps wire `--scope` into sync, push,
  finalize, read-only commands. Specifics decided per step.

**Helper placement** is deliberately open.

- `common.rs`, `init.rs`, or `scope.rs` / `scope_helpers.rs`

  SSH (scp-like and `ssh://`), and `~/…`/`$VAR`.

- `--repo-local` ⊕ `--dir` / `--owner` / `--private`.
- `--repo-remote` ⊕ `--owner` / `--private`.
- `NAME` and `--repo-remote` URL with disagreeing names.
- `--repo-local` without `NAME`.

**NAME derivation.** `--repo-remote` URL's last path segment
(trailing `.git` stripped) is used when positional `NAME` is
absent. NAME stays optional at the clap layer; runtime
errors cover the missing-required cases.

# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress

1. vc-x1 commands: support single and dual repo. Generalize via
   `--scope=app|other|both` across all commands; foundational
   for new commands and for retrofitting sync / push / finalize
   under one vocabulary. [60],[70]

## Todo

A markdown list of tasks to do in the near future, ordered
highest-priority first. Keep entries brief — 1-3 lines.
Detailed motivation, safety requirements, and ordering belong
in `notes/chores-NN.md` design subsections; link via `[N]` ref.

Items use lazy numbering — every entry begins with `1. `; the
markdown renderer auto-numbers them, so reorder/insert without
renumbering. Reference by displayed number ("let's work on #3").
1. vc-x1 push: `--scope=app|claude|both` flag. Applies the
   generalized convention; warn on scope/WC mismatch. [57],[60]
1. vc-x1 push: `--squash` flag. Squashes WC into `@-` via
   `--ignore-immutable` and force-pushes; needs
   `--force-with-lease`-equivalent + state-sanity preflight in
   place first. [57]
1. vc-x1 push: `--message-file PATH` flag. Git-style commit
   message file (first line = title, blank, rest = body).
   Alternative to `--title` + `--body`. [58]
1. Mirror `--check` / `--no-check` onto `vc-x1 push` (forwards
   through to the preflight `vc-x1 sync` invocation).
   0.37.1 hard-codes `--check`; default stays `--check`.
1. Add `status` (alias `st`) subcommand: `jj st` across both
   repos in one shot. Uses `--scope` from day one. Natural
   home for the working-copy signal called out in [54].
1. Add `validate-repo` subcommand: diagnostic that runs all
   `verify_*` checks (tracking, push state freshness, ochid
   integrity, conflicts, config sanity, working-copy state)
   and reports per-check pass/fail. Exit code = number of
   failed checks. Implementation: promote
   `verify_state_sanity` / `verify_completion_sanity` from
   push.rs to `common.rs`. [69]
1. sync: surface working-copy state in the up-to-date summary
   (per-repo pending-files count or compact stat). Wording-only
   fix shipped in 0.37.1; this is the design+impl. [54]
1. bm-track silent-when-clean refinement. Print on entry/exit
   only when state isn't fully tracked or when exit state
   differs from entry. [62]
1. "Oh shit" revert — post-success undo via `.vc-x1-ops/`
   anchor dir. Idea-stage; every repo-mutating command drops a
   pre-op snapshot, `vc-x1 undo` restores both repos. [57]
1. vc-x1 test-fixture should refuse `--path` values that resolve
   inside the current workspace root (error or warn). Dogfood
   surfaced this: `--path ./tf-1` inside the repo let jj
   snapshot the fixture's bare-git remotes — 56-file noise blob.
1. Restructure templates: replace separate `vc-template-x1` +
   `vc-template-x1.claude` repos with a single `vc-template-x1`
   that has `.claude/` as a subdir (covers `LICENSE-*` etc. for
   both sides in one place). Updates to `vc-x1 init` / `clone`
   needed for the new layout.
1. Source-code design ref sweep + CLAUDE.md codification:
   adopt section-name + `blob/main/...` URL pattern for source
   code refs to designs; codify in CLAUDE.md alongside the
   existing markdown ref conventions. Sweep targets:
   src/push.rs lines 4, 121, 645, 1219. [68]
1. Richer bookmark enumeration: per-bookmark remote presence + tracking status [52]
1. Per-line/per-thread runtime log points (future, maybe) [36]
1. Add Windows symlink support via `std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir` [37]
1. Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility
1. Add -p, --parents, -c, --children so parent and child counts can be asymmetric
1. Add integration tests in tests/ for subcommands using temp jj repos (tempfile crate)
1. Fix .claude repo history: dev0 through dev2 sessions squashed into wrong commit [4],[5]
1. Add `vc-x1 setup` subcommand: completions install, .claude repo init, symlink setup [27]
1. Add dynamic revision completion via `ArgValueCompleter` (jj doesn't complete revsets either) [28],[29]

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

- CLAUDE.md refresh + memory migration (0.36.1) [49]
- Lift sync's inline test harness into shared `test_helpers` module (0.36.2) [51]
- Sync improvements: -R flag + quieter dry-run + sync-before-work discipline (0.36.3) [50]
- push subcommand scaffolding: flag surface, Stage enum, stub (0.37.0-0) [48]
- push state machine: state file, --status/--restart/--from, stage stubs (0.37.0-1) [48]
- push real stage bodies + jj-op snapshot rollback (0.37.0-2) [48]
- push integration tests + workspace-root refactor (0.37.0-3) [48]
- push interactivity: review prompt, $EDITOR, message persistence (0.37.0-4) [48]
- push polish: --dry-run, --step, non-tty detection, gitignore warning (0.37.0-5) [48]
- push docs + workflow migration — CLAUDE.md rewrite + README section (0.37.0) [48]
- First-dogfood polish for push: editor template, gitignore-fatal, sync --check, log prefix, quieter subprocess (0.37.1) [53]
- Temporary bookmark-tracking diagnostic probe on command entry/exit (0.37.2) [55]
- Fix bm-track bugs + rename + promote to permanent (0.37.3) [56]
- Capture squash-mode + scope design for push (0.37.4) [57]
- Capture --message-file design for push (0.37.5) [58]
- CLAUDE.md polish: markdown-anchor rule, shell-path brevity, state-file clearing, late-changes recipe trimmed (0.37.6) [59]
- Notes restructure: chores-06 + trim long todo entries (0.37.7) [64]
- Scope design refinements (0.37.8) [65]
- Bookmark tracking verification: shared helper + tests (0.38.0-0) [66]
- Bookmark tracking verification: wire into setup commands (0.38.0-1) [66]
- Bookmark tracking verification: wire into preflight commands (0.38.0-2) [66]
- Bookmark tracking verification: cycle close-out + dogfood validation (0.38.0) [66]
- Push hardening: state-sanity preflight on resume (0.39.0-0) [67]
- Push hardening: honest completion via post-completion verification (0.39.0-1) [67]
- Push hardening: cycle close-out, 0.39.0-2 skipped (0.39.0) [67]

content.text:
All clean. The doc-style overhaul didn't affect any logic — 233 tests still pass.

## Proposed CLAUDE.md refinement

The user-facing convention you've now stated twice (function docs + chore descriptions) belongs in CLAUDE.md so it survives across sessions. The existing `### Doc comments on every file, function, and method` section says "Keep them brief — one sentence of purpose is often enough" but doesn't pin down the *shape*. I'd append a paragraph there:

```markdown
**Shape:** short intro (1–3 sentences, shorter is better),
then a `-` bullet list for any structured details. Avoid
long prose paragraphs — they read as a wall of text and
hide the structure that bullets make scannable. This
applies to:

- Module/function/struct/field doc comments in `.rs` files.
- Chore descriptions in `notes/chores-NN.md`.
- Todo entries in `notes/todo.md`.
```

That'd live under "Doc comments on every file, function, and method" with a small section break, or as a new top-level subsection under `## Code Conventions`. Either placement works — the content's the load-bearing part.

OK to add to CLAUDE.md as part of the `-1` commit? (Logically tied — both shipped together.)

input.command: grep -n "^### \|^## Code Conventions\|^## " CLAUDE.md | head -25

### Doc comments on every file, function, and method

Every `.rs` file must begin with a `//!` module docstring. Every
function and method must have a `///` doc comment. Keep them brief —
one sentence of purpose is often enough; the discipline is that the
comment exists, not that it be long.

This is a deliberate override of the generic "write no comments"
default that applies to inline `//` comments. Doc comments on the
module / item surface are expected; inline explanatory comments
inside function bodies remain discouraged unless they capture a
non-obvious WHY.

### `// OK: …` comments on `unwrap*` calls (Rust)

### Doc comments on every file, function, and method

Every `.rs` file must begin with a `//!` module docstring. Every
function and method must have a `///` doc comment. Keep them brief —
one sentence of purpose is often enough; the discipline is that the
comment exists, not that it be long.

module / item surface are expected; inline explanatory comments

input.new_string:
### Doc comments on every file, function, and method

Every `.rs` file must begin with a `//!` module docstring. Every
function and method must have a `///` doc comment. Keep them brief —
one sentence of purpose is often enough; the discipline is that the
comment exists, not that it be long.

This is a deliberate override of the generic "write no comments"
default that applies to inline `//` comments. Doc comments on the
module / item surface are expected; inline explanatory comments
inside function bodies remain discouraged unless they capture a
non-obvious WHY.

**Shape:** short intro (1–3 sentences, shorter is better), then
a `-` bullet list for any structured details. Avoid long prose
paragraphs — they read as a wall of text and hide the structure
that bullets make scannable. Same shape applies to:

- Module / function / struct / field doc comments in `.rs` files.
- Chore descriptions in `notes/chores-NN.md`.
- Todo entries in `notes/todo.md` (when an item needs more than
  one line of detail; pure one-liners are still fine).

### `// OK: …` comments on `unwrap*` calls (Rust)

          - Project + session at `<PARENT>/<NAME>/` and

          default). - Other URLs → caller pre-creates the remote; init only

message.content:
That's ugly, to much white space:
wink@3900x 26-04-25T01:20:13.833Z:~/data/prgs/rust/vc-x1 ((main))
$ vc-x1 init --help
vc-x1 0.40.0-1

Create a new dual-repo project

Usage: vc-x1 init [OPTIONS] [NAME]

Arguments:
  [NAME]
          Project name (directory and GitHub repo name).
          
          - Optional with `--repo-remote` (derived from URL's last path segment,
          trailing `.git` stripped).
          
          - Required with `--repo-local` and in default mode.
          
          - Fatal if both given and they disagree.

Options:
      --owner <OWNER>
          GitHub user or organization.
          
          - Only meaningful in default mode.
          
          - Fatal with `--repo-local` or `--repo-remote`.

  -v, --verbose...
          Verbose output: -v debug, -vv trace

      --dir <DIR>
          Parent directory to create the project under [default: cwd].
          
          - Fatal with `--repo-local` (that flag supplies the parent).

      --log <LOG>
          Log file path (captures all levels)

      --private
          Create private GitHub repos (default: public).
          
          - Only meaningful in default mode.
          
          - Fatal with `--repo-local` or `--repo-remote`.

      --dry-run
          Dry run — show what would be done without executing

      --push-retries <PUSH_RETRIES>
          Max push retries after repo creation [default: 5]
          
          [default: 5]

      --push-retry-delay <PUSH_RETRY_DELAY>
          Seconds between push retries [default: 3]
          
          [default: 3]

      --use-template <CODE[,BOT]>
          Seed both repos from template directories.
          
          Value is `CODE[,BOT]`. If `BOT` is omitted, defaults to the sibling
          directory `<CODE>.claude` (file-name concat, not path join — they are
          not nested). Non-hidden contents are copied recursively; hidden
          entries (names starting with `.`) are skipped since init creates the
          repo's own hidden files. If either template has a `README.md`, its
          first line is rewritten to `# <repo-name>`.

      --repo-local <PARENT>
          Fixture mode — fully self-contained local workspace.
          
          Value is a single parent directory.
          
          - Project + session at `<PARENT>/<NAME>/` and
          `<PARENT>/<NAME>/.claude/`.
          
          - Bare origins at `<PARENT>/remote-code.git` and
          `<PARENT>/remote-claude.git`.
          
          - No `gh`, no network.
          
          - Supports `~/…`, `$VAR`, `${VAR}`; relative paths against cwd.
          
          - Exclusive with `--repo-remote`, `--dir`, `--owner`, `--private`.

      --repo-remote <URL>
          Override the `origin` URL stem (single value).
          
          - Session URL derived: insert `.claude` before `.git` suffix, or
          append if no `.git`.
          
          - GitHub URLs → `gh repo create` (same as default).
          
          - Other URLs → caller pre-creates the remote; init only pushes.
          
          - Supports `~/…`, `$VAR`, `${VAR}` in path-shaped values.
          
          - Exclusive with `--repo-local`, `--owner`, `--private`.

  -h, --help
          Print help (see a summary with '-h')

  -V, --version
          Print version

`--help` output. Clap reflows by default and collapses bullets

any field whose doc comment uses bullets so each `- …` lands on
its own line in the rendered help.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress

1. Generalize `--scope=app|other|both` so commands work on
   single, dual, or POR workspaces. [60],[70]
   - Foundational for new commands.
   - Retrofits sync / push / finalize under one vocabulary.

## Todo

A markdown list of tasks to do in the near future, ordered
highest-priority first. Keep entries brief — 1-3 lines.
Detailed motivation, safety requirements, and ordering belong
in `notes/chores-NN.md` design subsections; link via `[N]` ref.

Items use lazy numbering — every entry begins with `1. `; the
markdown renderer auto-numbers them, so reorder/insert without
renumbering. Reference by displayed number ("let's work on #3").
1. vc-x1 push: `--scope=app|claude|both` flag. Applies the
   generalized convention; warn on scope/WC mismatch. [57],[60]
1. vc-x1 push: `--squash` flag. Squashes WC into `@-` via
   `--ignore-immutable` and force-pushes; needs
   `--force-with-lease`-equivalent + state-sanity preflight in
   place first. [57]
1. vc-x1 push: `--message-file PATH` flag. Git-style commit
   message file (first line = title, blank, rest = body).
   Alternative to `--title` + `--body`. [58]
1. Mirror `--check` / `--no-check` onto `vc-x1 push` (forwards
   through to the preflight `vc-x1 sync` invocation).
   0.37.1 hard-codes `--check`; default stays `--check`.
1. Add `status` (alias `st`) subcommand: `jj st` across both
   repos in one shot. Uses `--scope` from day one. Natural
   home for the working-copy signal called out in [54].
1. Add `validate-repo` subcommand: diagnostic that runs all
   `verify_*` checks (tracking, push state freshness, ochid
   integrity, conflicts, config sanity, working-copy state)
   and reports per-check pass/fail. Exit code = number of
   failed checks. Implementation: promote
   `verify_state_sanity` / `verify_completion_sanity` from
   push.rs to `common.rs`. [69]
1. sync: surface working-copy state in the up-to-date summary
   (per-repo pending-files count or compact stat). Wording-only
   fix shipped in 0.37.1; this is the design+impl. [54]
1. bm-track silent-when-clean refinement. Print on entry/exit
   only when state isn't fully tracked or when exit state
   differs from entry. [62]
1. "Oh shit" revert — post-success undo via `.vc-x1-ops/`
   anchor dir. Idea-stage; every repo-mutating command drops a
   pre-op snapshot, `vc-x1 undo` restores both repos. [57]
1. vc-x1 test-fixture should refuse `--path` values that resolve
   inside the current workspace root (error or warn). Dogfood
   surfaced this: `--path ./tf-1` inside the repo let jj
   snapshot the fixture's bare-git remotes — 56-file noise blob.
1. Restructure templates: replace separate `vc-template-x1` +
   `vc-template-x1.claude` repos with a single `vc-template-x1`
   that has `.claude/` as a subdir (covers `LICENSE-*` etc. for
   both sides in one place). Updates to `vc-x1 init` / `clone`
   needed for the new layout.
1. Source-code design ref sweep + CLAUDE.md codification:
   adopt section-name + `blob/main/...` URL pattern for source
   code refs to designs; codify in CLAUDE.md alongside the
   existing markdown ref conventions. Sweep targets:
   src/push.rs lines 4, 121, 645, 1219. [68]
1. Richer bookmark enumeration: per-bookmark remote presence + tracking status [52]
1. Per-line/per-thread runtime log points (future, maybe) [36]
1. Add Windows symlink support via `std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir` [37]
1. Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility
1. Add -p, --parents, -c, --children so parent and child counts can be asymmetric
1. Add integration tests in tests/ for subcommands using temp jj repos (tempfile crate)
1. Fix .claude repo history: dev0 through dev2 sessions squashed into wrong commit [4],[5]
1. Add `vc-x1 setup` subcommand: completions install, .claude repo init, symlink setup [27]
1. Add dynamic revision completion via `ArgValueCompleter` (jj doesn't complete revsets either) [28],[29]

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

- CLAUDE.md refresh + memory migration (0.36.1) [49]
- Lift sync's inline test harness into shared `test_helpers` module (0.36.2) [51]
- Sync improvements: -R flag + quieter dry-run + sync-before-work discipline (0.36.3) [50]
- push subcommand scaffolding: flag surface, Stage enum, stub (0.37.0-0) [48]
- push state machine: state file, --status/--restart/--from, stage stubs (0.37.0-1) [48]
- push real stage bodies + jj-op snapshot rollback (0.37.0-2) [48]
- push integration tests + workspace-root refactor (0.37.0-3) [48]
- push interactivity: review prompt, $EDITOR, message persistence (0.37.0-4) [48]
- push polish: --dry-run, --step, non-tty detection, gitignore warning (0.37.0-5) [48]
- push docs + workflow migration — CLAUDE.md rewrite + README section (0.37.0) [48]
- First-dogfood polish for push: editor template, gitignore-fatal, sync --check, log prefix, quieter subprocess (0.37.1) [53]
- Temporary bookmark-tracking diagnostic probe on command entry/exit (0.37.2) [55]
- Fix bm-track bugs + rename + promote to permanent (0.37.3) [56]
- Capture squash-mode + scope design for push (0.37.4) [57]
- Capture --message-file design for push (0.37.5) [58]
- CLAUDE.md polish: markdown-anchor rule, shell-path brevity, state-file clearing, late-changes recipe trimmed (0.37.6) [59]
- Notes restructure: chores-06 + trim long todo entries (0.37.7) [64]
- Scope design refinements (0.37.8) [65]
- Bookmark tracking verification: shared helper + tests (0.38.0-0) [66]
- Bookmark tracking verification: wire into setup commands (0.38.0-1) [66]
- Bookmark tracking verification: wire into preflight commands (0.38.0-2) [66]
- Bookmark tracking verification: cycle close-out + dogfood validation (0.38.0) [66]
- Push hardening: state-sanity preflight on resume (0.39.0-0) [67]
- Push hardening: honest completion via post-completion verification (0.39.0-1) [67]
- Push hardening: cycle close-out, 0.39.0-2 skipped (0.39.0) [67]

content.text:
## Summary of step `-1` changes

**`src/init.rs`** (substantial — +1310/-161):
- Two new flags: `--repo-local <PARENT>` (fixture mode) and `--repo-remote <URL>` (single URL, name-from-URL derivation). Positional `NAME` now `Option<String>`.
- Tiered `Provisioner` dispatch (`LocalBareInit` / `GhCreate` / `ExternalPreExisting`) — default mode + `--repo-remote` for GitHub URLs run the same code path, so `vc-x1 init tf1` ≡ `vc-x1 init tf1 --owner winksaville` ≡ `vc-x1 init --repo-remote git@github.com:winksaville/tf1` (test-asserted).
- `InitPlan` struct + `plan_init` resolver enforces all ambiguity rules up front.
- 11 helpers: `expand_vars`, `is_remote_url`, `is_github_url`, `normalize_remote`, `normalize_local_parent`, `ensure_git_suffix`, `derive_session_url`, `derive_name_from_url`, `github_slug_from_url`, `split_slug`, `run_remote_step`.
- `--repo-local`/`--repo-remote` field docs use `#[arg(verbatim_doc_comment)]` so clap renders bullets cleanly.
- 17 new tests covering arg parsing, helpers, plan dispatch, ambiguity rejections, equivalence assertion.
- Documentary `debug!(…)` lines above each `run()` call.

**`Cargo.toml`** — `0.40.0-0` → `0.40.0-1`.

**`CLAUDE.md`** — appended doc-shape guidance (short intro + `-` bullets, applies to source docs / chores / todo entries) plus the `verbatim_doc_comment` clap-arg note.

**`notes/chores-06.md`** — rewrote `## Generalize --scope across commands (0.40.0)` in the new bullet style, swapped `-1`/`-2` step responsibilities with ordering rationale, appended `### 0.40.0-1: init remote decoupling` shipped subsection.

**`notes/todo.md`** — In Progress entry tightened to new style; added `[70]` ref; added new todo for the dry-run-skips-preflight observation surfaced during smoke testing.

**Verification:**
- `cargo fmt`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean.
- `cargo test` — 233 pass (216 → 233).
- `cargo install --path . --locked` clean.
- Smoke tests:
  - `vc-x1 init tf1 --repo-local /tmp/.../fixtures` → exact layout you specified (`fixtures/tf1`, `fixtures/remote-code.git`, `fixtures/remote-claude.git`); both bare repos got the initial commit; no `gh` invocations.
  - `vc-x1 init --repo-remote /tmp/.../tf2 --dir /tmp/...` → name "tf2" derived from URL; pre-existing bare paths picked up; `ExternalPreExisting` provisioner skipped any create step.
  - `--help` rendering: bullets land on their own lines with proper continuation indenting.

Work complete. Please review. On approval I'll prep the commit title and body for a second approval before pushing.

content: Goal: ship 0.40.0-1, decoupling vc-x1 init from gh via new --repo-local fixture mode and --repo-remote URL override, with unified Provisioner dispatch. Code, tests, docs, and smoke tests all done; awaiting your review approval before I propose the commit title and body.

content.text:
**Proposed commit title and body** (same in both repos; `vc-x1 push` adds the per-repo `ochid:` trailer):

**Title** (39 chars):
```
feat: init remote decoupling (0.40.0-1)
```

**Body:**
```
Decouple init from `gh repo create` so it can target any git
remote (local bare repo, file:///, SSH, HTTPS, etc.). Adds two
new flags plus a unified Provisioner dispatch that subsumes
the existing default mode. See chores-06 0.40.0-1 for details.

- src/init.rs: new flags --repo-local / --repo-remote;
  Provisioner enum + InitPlan + plan_init dispatcher; 11 new
  helpers; documentary debug! retrofit; 17 new tests.
- Cargo.toml: bump to 0.40.0-1.
- CLAUDE.md: doc-shape guidance (short intro + `-` bullets);
  verbatim_doc_comment note for clap-derive args.
- notes/chores-06.md: amend cycle steps (-1 ↔ -2 swap +
  ordering rationale); new "0.40.0-1: init remote decoupling"
  subsection.
- notes/todo.md: tighten In Progress entry to new style; add
  [70]; new todo for --dry-run preflight bypass.
```
